483	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 1000000 in
484	-- Deep nested localization instance synthesis (cf. `gf_torsion_reindex`) needs raised budgets.
485	set_option maxHeartbeats 4000000 in
486	-- The comparison-map (`ν`, `ψ`) + `aeval` assembly over stacked `Away` localizations is heavy.
487	/-- **L4 — Noether normalisation with clearing of denominators**
488	(`lem:gf_noether_clear_denominators`, Nitsure §4). For `A` a noetherian domain
489	with fraction field `K`, and `B` a finite-type `A`-algebra that is a domain with
490	`B_K = K ⊗_A B ≠ 0`, there exist `g ∈ A`, `g ≠ 0`, a number `n`, and an
491	injective `A_g`-algebra map `φ : A_g[X_1,…,X_n] → B_g` (the `b_j := φ(X_j)`
492	algebraically independent over `K`) such that `B_g` is module-finite over
493	`A_g[X_1,…,X_n]` through `φ`.
494	
495	Here `A_g := Localization.Away g`, `B_g := Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g)`,
496	and module-finiteness is taken with respect to the `A_g[X]`-module structure on
497	`B_g` induced by `φ`.
498	
499	Proof (Nitsure §4): apply Noether normalisation `exists_finite_inj_algHom_of_fg`
500	over the field `K` to the finite-type `K`-algebra `B_K`, obtaining algebraically
501	independent `b̄_j ∈ B_K` with `B_K` module-finite over `K[b̄_1,…,b̄_n]`. Each
502	`b̄_j` is `1 ⊗ b_j` for some `b_j ∈ B`; choosing a finite generating set and
503	finitely many integral-dependence equations and letting `g` be a common
504	denominator of their coefficients makes `B_g` module-finite over
505	`A_g[b_1,…,b_n]`. -/
506	theorem exists_localizationAway_finite_mvPolynomial
507	    (A B : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
508	    [CommRing B] [IsDomain B] [Algebra A B] [Algebra.FiniteType A B]
509	    (hBK : Nontrivial (TensorProduct A (FractionRing A) B)) :
510	    ∃ (n : ℕ) (g : A) (_ : g ≠ 0)
511	      (algBg : Algebra (Localization.Away g) (Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g)))
512	      (φ : MvPolynomial (Fin n) (Localization.Away g)
513	            →ₐ[Localization.Away g] Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g)),
514	      Function.Injective φ ∧
515	      (letI := φ.toAlgebra;
516	        Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin n) (Localization.Away g))
517	          (Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g))) ∧
518	      -- The chosen `A_g`-algebra structure on `B_g` is compatible with the
519	      -- canonical `A → B → B_g` tower: `A_g → B_g` extends `A → B → B_g`. This
520	      -- pins `algBg` (otherwise an arbitrary instance) to the localisation map,
521	      -- which the cascade needs to bridge `LocalizedModule (powers g) B ≃ B_g`.
522	      (letI := algBg; ∀ a : A,
523	        algebraMap (Localization.Away g) (Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g))
524	            (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) a)
525	          = algebraMap B (Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g)) (algebraMap A B a)) := by
526	  classical
527	  -- Step 1 (Nitsure §4): Noether normalisation over the fraction field
528	  -- `K = Frac A`, applied to the finite-type `K`-algebra `B_K = K ⊗_A B`. This
529	  -- yields algebraically independent generators (encoded by the injective AlgHom
530	  -- `gK`) over which `B_K` is module-finite.
531	  haveI : Nontrivial (TensorProduct A (FractionRing A) B) := hBK
532	  haveI : Algebra.FiniteType (FractionRing A) (TensorProduct A (FractionRing A) B) :=
533	    inferInstance
534	  obtain ⟨s, gK, hgK_inj, hgK_fin⟩ :=
535	    exists_finite_inj_algHom_of_fg (FractionRing A) (TensorProduct A (FractionRing A) B)
536	  -- `gK : MvPolynomial (Fin s) K →ₐ[K] B_K`, injective and module-finite.
537	  --
538	  -- ===================================================================
539	  -- iter-018 foundation (proved below, `g`-independent and reusable). L4 was
540	  -- closed iter-021; there is no `sorry` here. The roadmap comment that follows
541	  -- documents the denominator-clearing assembly now realised below.
542	  -- ===================================================================
543	  --
544	  -- (F1) `A ↪ B`. Nontriviality of `B_K = K ⊗_A B` forces `algebraMap A B`
545	  -- injective: if `0 ≠ a ∈ A` had `algebraMap A B a = 0`, then `a` acts as `0`
546	  -- on every pure tensor (`a • (k ⊗ b) = k ⊗ (a • b) = k ⊗ 0 = 0`) yet `a` acts
547	  -- invertibly through the unit `algebraMap A K a`, forcing `B_K` subsingleton.
548	  have hAB : Function.Injective (algebraMap A B) := by
549	    rw [injective_iff_map_eq_zero]
550	    intro a ha
551	    by_contra h0
552	    have hunit : IsUnit (algebraMap A (FractionRing A) a) :=
553	      IsLocalization.map_units (FractionRing A)
554	        (⟨a, mem_nonZeroDivisors_of_ne_zero h0⟩ : nonZeroDivisors A)
555	    have hsub : ∀ z : TensorProduct A (FractionRing A) B, z = 0 := by
556	      intro z
557	      have haz : a • z = 0 := by
558	        induction z using TensorProduct.induction_on with
559	        | zero => simp
560	        | tmul k b =>
561	          rw [← TensorProduct.tmul_smul, Algebra.smul_def, ha, zero_mul, TensorProduct.tmul_zero]
562	        | add x y hx hy => rw [smul_add, hx, hy, add_zero]
563	      have h2 : (algebraMap A (FractionRing A) a) • z = 0 := by
564	        rw [IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul]; exact haz
565	      exact (hunit.smul_eq_zero).mp h2
566	    haveI : Subsingleton (TensorProduct A (FractionRing A) B) :=
567	      ⟨fun x y => by rw [hsub x, hsub y]⟩
568	    exact (not_subsingleton _) ‹_›
569	  -- (F2) `B_K = K ⊗_A B` is the localisation of `B` at the image of the non-zero
570	  -- divisors of `A` (`IsLocalization.tensorRight`). This is the structural fact
571	  -- that lets us clear denominators of `gK (X j) ∈ B_K` down into `B` (and then
572	  -- into `B_g`), and that identifies the canonical maps `B → B_g → B_K`.
573	  letI : Algebra B (TensorProduct A (FractionRing A) B) := Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra
574	  haveI hloc : IsLocalization (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid B (nonZeroDivisors A))
575	      (TensorProduct A (FractionRing A) B) :=
576	    IsLocalization.tensorRight (FractionRing A) (nonZeroDivisors A)
577	  -- (F3) A single common denominator `g0 ∈ A`, `g0 ≠ 0`, clearing all `s`
578	  -- generators `gK (X j)` into `B`: for each `j` there is `β_j ∈ B` with
579	  -- `algebraMap B B_K β_j = (algebraMap A B g0) • gK (X j)`.
580	  obtain ⟨den, hden⟩ := IsLocalization.exist_integer_multiples
581	    (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid B (nonZeroDivisors A)) (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin s))
582	    (fun j => gK (MvPolynomial.X j))
583	  obtain ⟨g0, hg0mem, hg0eq⟩ := den.2
584	  have hg0ne : g0 ≠ 0 := nonZeroDivisors.ne_zero hg0mem
585	  -- (F4) `B_K` is integral over the polynomial subring `gK (K[X])` (module-finite
586	  -- ⟹ integral), so every element — in particular the image of each `A`-algebra
587	  -- generator of `B` — satisfies a monic equation with coefficients in `K[X]`.
588	  letI := gK.toAlgebra
589	  haveI hmf : Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin s) (FractionRing A))
590	      (TensorProduct A (FractionRing A) B) := hgK_fin
591	  haveI hint : Algebra.IsIntegral (MvPolynomial (Fin s) (FractionRing A))
592	      (TensorProduct A (FractionRing A) B) := Algebra.IsIntegral.of_finite _ _
593	  -- (F5) A finite `A`-algebra generating set `σ` of `B` (hence of `B_g` over
594	  -- `A_g`, and so of `B_g` over `A_g[X]` once `φ` is in hand).
595	  obtain ⟨σ, hσ⟩ : ∃ σ : Finset B, Algebra.adjoin A (↑σ : Set B) = ⊤ :=
596	    (inferInstance : Algebra.FiniteType A B).out
597	  -- (F6) the generators `gK (X j)` are algebraically independent over `K`
598	  -- (`hgK_inj`, since `gK = aeval (gK ∘ X)`). Restricting scalars along the
599	  -- injection `A_g ↪ K` will descend this to independence over `A_g`, which is

300	        ((D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2)).map (g p.1 p.2).inv ≫
301	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
302	        (show (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2 = D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1 by
303	          rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition p.1 p.2)).hom.app
304	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))
305	  let a : P ⟶ ∏ᶜ Qfun := Pi.lift fun p => Pi.π _ p.1 ≫ aComp p
306	  let b : P ⟶ ∏ᶜ Qfun := Pi.lift fun p => Pi.π _ p.2 ≫ bComp p
307	  equalizer a b

309	/-! ### Pullback of free sheaves along an arbitrary scheme morphism
310	
311	The functor of points (`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor`) acts on morphisms by
312	pullback, and for that one needs `f^* (O^n) ≅ O^n` for an *arbitrary* scheme morphism
313	`f`. Mathlib's `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` supplies this only when the underlying
314	site functor `Opens.map f.base` is `Final`; the previous chapters discharged that `Final`
315	hypothesis only for open immersions and isomorphisms. The first lemma below removes the
316	restriction entirely: `Opens.map f.base` is `Final` for *every* scheme morphism, because
317	the structured-arrow category over any open `V` has a terminal object (the whole space
318	`⊤`). With that in hand, `pullbackFreeIso` and `pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` hold for all
319	morphisms. -/
320	
321	/-- For an arbitrary scheme morphism `φ`, the site functor `Opens.map φ.base` is `Final`:
322	over any open `V` of the target the structured-arrow category `{U : V ≤ φ⁻¹ U}` has the
323	terminal object `U = ⊤`, hence is connected. This is the missing ingredient that makes
324	`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit`/`pullbackObjFreeIso` applicable to *every* morphism,
325	not just to open immersions. Project-local. -/
326	lemma opensMap_final {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) :
327	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base).Final := by
328	  constructor
329	  intro V
330	  set top : StructuredArrow V (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base) :=
331	    StructuredArrow.mk (Y := (⊤ : T.Opens)) (homOfLE le_top)
332	  haveI : Nonempty (StructuredArrow V (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base)) := ⟨top⟩
333	  apply zigzag_isConnected
334	  intro s t
335	  have hs : s ⟶ top := StructuredArrow.homMk (homOfLE le_top) (Subsingleton.elim _ _)
336	  have ht : t ⟶ top := StructuredArrow.homMk (homOfLE le_top) (Subsingleton.elim _ _)
337	  exact Relation.ReflTransGen.trans
338	    (Relation.ReflTransGen.single (Or.inl ⟨hs⟩))
339	    (Relation.ReflTransGen.single (Or.inr ⟨ht⟩))
340	
341	/-- **Pullback of a free sheaf of modules is free, for any scheme morphism**: for
342	`φ : T' ⟶ T` and an index type `I`, `φ^*(O_T^{⊕I}) ≅ O_{T'}^{⊕I}`. Built from
343	`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` once `opensMap_final` supplies the `Final` instance.
344	Project-local. -/
345	noncomputable def pullbackFreeIso {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) (I : Type u) :
346	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I)
347	      ≅ SheafOfModules.free (R := T'.ringCatSheaf) I := by
348	  haveI := opensMap_final φ
349	  exact SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso φ.toRingCatSheafHom I
350	
351	/-- **Pullback preserves rank-`d` local freeness.** If `M` is locally free of rank `d` on
352	`T`, then `φ^* M` is locally free of rank `d` on `T'`, for any scheme morphism `φ`. The
353	chart cover `{U i}` of `T` trivialising `M` pulls back to the cover `{φ⁻¹ U i}` of `T'`;
354	on each member the restriction of `φ^* M` is identified with the pulled-back chart-free
355	sheaf via the pseudofunctor comparison `pullbackComp`, the factorisation
356	`φ ∘ (φ⁻¹ U i).ι = (φ ∣_ U i) ≫ (U i).ι` (`morphismRestrict_ι`), and `pullbackFreeIso`.
357	Project-local. -/
358	lemma pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) {M : T.Modules}
359	    {d : ℕ} (h : SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank M d) :
360	    SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj M) d := by
361	  obtain ⟨ι, U, hcover, hloc⟩ := h
362	  refine ⟨ι, fun i => φ ⁻¹ᵁ (U i), Scheme.Hom.iSup_preimage_eq_top φ hcover, ?_⟩
363	  intro i
364	  obtain ⟨e⟩ := hloc i
365	  exact ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (φ ⁻¹ᵁ (U i)).ι φ).app M ≪≫
366	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (morphismRestrict_ι φ (U i)).symm).app M ≪≫
367	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (φ ∣_ (U i)) (U i).ι).app M).symm ≪≫
368	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (φ ∣_ (U i))).mapIso e ≪≫
369	    pullbackFreeIso (φ ∣_ (U i)) (ULift.{u} (Fin d))⟩
370	
371	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

375	/-! ## The universal quotient sheaf and the tautological quotient -/
376	
377	/-- The **universal quotient sheaf** `U` on `Gr(d,r)` (`def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf`):
378	the rank-`d` locally free sheaf obtained by gluing the free rank-`d` chart sheaves
379	`O_{U^I}^d` along the bundle transition cocycle `g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)⁻¹`.
380	
381	NOTE: `Scheme.Modules.glue` has landed (axiom-clean, via the descent equalizer of
382	pushforwards). The remaining obligation is the **GL_d bundle transition cocycle**, which
383	is net-new infrastructure not reachable from `glue` alone:
384	`universalQuotient d r := Scheme.Modules.glue (theGlueData d r)`
385	`  (fun I => SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I.1).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) g hC1 hC2`
386	where `g I J : (pullback (chartIncl I J))^* O^d ≅ (pullback (t I J ≫ chartIncl J I))^* O^d` is
387	the matrix automorphism of free sheaves induced by `universalMinorInv I J = (X^I_J)⁻¹`
388	(built like `chartQuotientMap`: `scalarEnd` of the matrix entries over the overlap ring,
389	assembled over the rank-`d` biproduct, conjugated by `pullbackFreeIso`), and `hC1`/`hC2` are
390	the module-level self-identity and triple-overlap multiplicativity — the `GL_d`-valued
391	cocycle, transported via `pullbackBaseChangeTransport`/`glueData_bridge_*`. -/
392	noncomputable def universalQuotient (d r : ℕ) : (scheme d r).Modules :=
393	  sorry
394	
395	/-- The **tautological quotient** `u : O^r ↠ U` (`def:tautological_quotient`): the global
396	surjection assembled from the chart quotients `u^I` (`chartQuotientMap`), compatible with
397	the bundle gluing of `universalQuotient`.
398	
399	NOTE: rides on `universalQuotient` (hence on the bundle cocycle, the only remaining gap;
400	`Scheme.Modules.glue` itself has landed). Assembled from the chart quotients `u^I`
401	(`chartQuotientMap`) glued along the same cocycle via `Scheme.Modules.glueHom`. -/
402	noncomputable def tautologicalQuotient (d r : ℕ) :
403	    SheafOfModules.free (R := (scheme d r).ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶ universalQuotient d r :=
404	  sorry

406	/-! ## The functor of points and the universal property
407	
408	The Grassmannian functor sends a scheme `T` to the set of *equivalence classes* of
409	rank-`d` quotients `q : O_T^r ↠ F` with `F` locally free of rank `d`. We encode a single
410	such quotient as the structure `RankQuotient r d T`, the equivalence as `RankQuotient.Rel`
411	(an isomorphism of the targets commuting with the quotient maps), and the functor's value
412	at `T` as the quotient `Quotient (rqSetoid r d T)`. The pullback action `rqPullback`
413	together with `pullbackFreeIso`/`pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` (and the fact that the
414	pullback functor preserves epimorphisms, being a left adjoint) realise functoriality.
415	
416	Because a sheaf of modules `F : T.Modules` is a large object, this quotient lives in
417	`Type 1` (not `Type 0` as the original scaffold signature stated); the corrected universe
418	is the only change to the pinned signature, and it is forced — the substantive content is
419	unchanged. -/
420	
421	/-- A **rank-`d` quotient of `O_T^r`** on a scheme `T`: a sheaf of modules `F` on `T`,
422	locally free of rank `d`, together with an epimorphism `q : O_T^r ↠ F`. This is the
423	unbundled datum whose equivalence classes form the value of the Grassmannian functor. -/
424	structure RankQuotient (r d : ℕ) (T : Scheme.{0}) where
425	  /-- The quotient sheaf. -/
426	  F : T.Modules
427	  /-- The quotient map out of the trivial rank-`r` bundle. -/
428	  q : SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶ F
429	  /-- The quotient map is an epimorphism (surjective). -/
430	  epi : Epi q
431	  /-- The quotient sheaf is locally free of rank `d`. -/
432	  locFree : SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank F d
433	
434	/-- Two rank-`d` quotients are **equivalent** when there is an isomorphism of the targets
435	commuting with the quotient maps (equivalently, when the kernels of the quotient maps
436	coincide). -/
437	def RankQuotient.Rel {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} (x y : RankQuotient r d T) : Prop :=
438	  ∃ f : x.F ≅ y.F, x.q ≫ f.hom = y.q
439	
440	lemma RankQuotient.rel_refl {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} (x : RankQuotient r d T) : x.Rel x :=
441	  ⟨Iso.refl _, Category.comp_id _⟩
442	
443	lemma RankQuotient.rel_symm {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} {x y : RankQuotient r d T}
444	    (h : x.Rel y) : y.Rel x := by
445	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
446	  exact ⟨f.symm, by rw [Iso.symm_hom, Iso.comp_inv_eq]; exact hf.symm⟩
447	
448	lemma RankQuotient.rel_trans {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} {x y z : RankQuotient r d T}
449	    (h1 : x.Rel y) (h2 : y.Rel z) : x.Rel z := by
450	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h1; obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := h2
451	  -- term-mode (the `T.Modules` def-diamond blocks positional category `rw`)
452	  exact ⟨f ≪≫ g,
453	    (congrArg (x.q ≫ ·) (Iso.trans_hom f g)).trans <|
454	      (Category.assoc x.q f.hom g.hom).symm.trans <|
455	        (congrArg (· ≫ g.hom) hf).trans hg⟩
456	
457	/-- The equivalence-of-quotients setoid on `RankQuotient r d T`. -/
458	instance rqSetoid (r d : ℕ) (T : Scheme.{0}) : Setoid (RankQuotient r d T) where
459	  r := RankQuotient.Rel
460	  iseqv := ⟨RankQuotient.rel_refl, RankQuotient.rel_symm, RankQuotient.rel_trans⟩
461	
462	/-- The **pullback action** on a rank-`d` quotient: pull the target sheaf and quotient map
463	back along `ψ`, re-presenting the source as the trivial bundle via `pullbackFreeIso`. The
464	result is again an epimorphism (pullback preserves epis) onto a rank-`d` locally free sheaf
465	(`pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank`). -/
466	noncomputable def rqPullback {r d : ℕ} {T' T : Scheme.{0}} (ψ : T' ⟶ T)
467	    (x : RankQuotient r d T) : RankQuotient r d T' where
468	  F := (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).obj x.F
469	  q := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map x.q
470	  epi :=
471	    -- fully explicit: the def-diamond on `T.Modules` blocks `Epi`-instance search, so
472	    -- `x.epi` is threaded through `map_epi`/`epi_comp` by hand
473	    @CategoryTheory.epi_comp _ _ _ _ _
474	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv inferInstance
475	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map x.q)
476	      (@CategoryTheory.Functor.map_epi _ _ _ _ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ) inferInstance _ _
477	        x.q x.epi)
478	  locFree := Scheme.Modules.pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank ψ x.locFree
479	
480	/-- The pullback action respects the equivalence relation, hence descends to quotients. -/
481	lemma rqPullback_rel {r d : ℕ} {T' T : Scheme.{0}} (ψ : T' ⟶ T)
482	    {x y : RankQuotient r d T} (h : x.Rel y) :
483	    (rqPullback ψ x).Rel (rqPullback ψ y) := by
484	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
485	  refine ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).mapIso f, ?_⟩
486	  change ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map x.q) ≫
487	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map f.hom
488	    = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map y.q
489	  rw [Category.assoc, ← (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map_comp]
490	  exact congrArg
491	    (fun m => (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map m) hf
492	
493	end AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian
494	
495	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
496	
497	/-- **Unit coherence (`map_id` keystone, `lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id`).** The
498	Mathlib free-pullback comparison `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit` at the identity
499	morphism agrees, on the unit sheaf, with the scheme-level pseudofunctor identity
500	`Scheme.Modules.pullbackId`. Project-local: bridges `pullbackObjFreeIso` to the
501	pseudofunctor `pullbackId`. -/
502	lemma pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id {T : Scheme.{u}} :
503	    SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (𝟙 T))
504	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId T).hom.app (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf) := by
505	  rw [← SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_symm_unitToPushforwardObjUnit,
506	    Equiv.symm_apply_eq, Adjunction.homEquiv_unit]
507	  have h := CategoryTheory.unit_conjugateEquiv Adjunction.id
508	    (SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (𝟙 T)))
509	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId T).hom (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf)
510	  simp only [Adjunction.id_unit, NatTrans.id_app, Functor.id_obj] at h
511	  rw [← h]
512	  -- term-mode `id_comp` (positional `rw [Category.id_comp]` hits the `T.Modules` instance diamond)
513	  refine Eq.trans ?_ (Category.id_comp _).symm
514	  -- the `conjugateEquiv` term sits in unfolded `SheafOfModules` form; bridge to the
515	  -- scheme-level pseudofunctor coherence by defeq, then it equals `(pushforwardId).inv`.
516	  have key : (CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv Adjunction.id
517	        (SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (𝟙 T)))
518	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId T).hom)
519	      = (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId T).inv :=
520	    Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom T
521	  rw [key]
522	  ext Y
523	  -- both sides evaluate the unit section `1` through identity-like maps
524	  rfl
525	
526	/-- **Free coherence (`map_id`).** `pullbackFreeIso (𝟙 T) I` agrees, on the free sheaf,
527	with the pseudofunctor identity `pullbackId`. Reduces to `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id` by
528	coproduct extensionality (`free = ∐ unit`). Project-local. -/
529	lemma pullbackFreeIso_id {T : Scheme.{u}} (I : Type u) :
530	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (𝟙 T) I).hom
531	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId T).hom.app (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I) := by
532	  haveI := Scheme.Modules.opensMap_final (𝟙 T)
533	  -- Use the `SheafOfModules.pullback` form in the cofan: the `Scheme.Modules.pullback` wrapper
534	  -- triggers a universe-polymorphism trap in the `PreservesColimit` instance search
535	  -- (memory `gf-seam1-1b1c-done`); the two forms are defeq, bridged by the explicit-type `have`s.
536	  refine Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext (isColimitCofanMkObjOfIsColimit
537	    (SheafOfModules.pullback (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (𝟙 T))) _ _
538	    (SheafOfModules.isColimitFreeCofan (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I)) _ _ (fun i => ?_)
539	  simp only [cofan_mk_inj]
540	  -- Pure term-mode chain (positional `rw`/`simp` fail under the `SheafOfModules`/`X.Modules`
541	  -- instance diamond — they cannot match identical-printing terms with different implicit
542	  -- instance arguments; `exact`/`Eq.trans` unify up to defeq, which is what is needed here).
543	  -- LHS: free-pullback comparison.  Then transport the unit coherence through `· ≫ ιFree i`,
544	  -- and finally undo by naturality of `pullbackId.hom` (RHS).
545	  exact (SheafOfModules.pullback_map_ιFree_comp_pullbackObjFreeIso_hom
546	        (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (𝟙 T)) i).trans
547	      ((congrArg (· ≫ SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := T.ringCatSheaf) i) pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id).trans
548	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackId T).hom.naturality
549	          (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := T.ringCatSheaf) i)).symm)
550	
551	/-- **Mate compatibility of `homEquiv`.** For adjunctions `adj₁ : L₁ ⊣ R₁`, `adj₂ : L₂ ⊣ R₂`
552	and a natural transformation `α : L₂ ⟶ L₁`, transposing `α.app c ≫ f` under `adj₂` equals
553	transposing `f` under `adj₁` post-composed with the conjugate transformation
554	(`CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α`) evaluated at `d`. Project-local; derived from
555	`CategoryTheory.unit_conjugateEquiv` + naturality of the conjugate transformation. -/
556	lemma homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app {𝒞 𝒟 : Type*} [CategoryTheory.Category 𝒞]
557	    [CategoryTheory.Category 𝒟] {L₁ L₂ : 𝒞 ⥤ 𝒟} {R₁ R₂ : 𝒟 ⥤ 𝒞}
558	    (adj₁ : L₁ ⊣ R₁) (adj₂ : L₂ ⊣ R₂) (α : L₂ ⟶ L₁) {c : 𝒞} {d : 𝒟}
559	    (f : L₁.obj c ⟶ d) :
560	    adj₂.homEquiv c d (α.app c ≫ f)
561	      = adj₁.homEquiv c d f ≫ (CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).app d := by
562	  -- `rw` is unreliable at locating these right-associated sub-composites, so we assemble the
563	  -- proof entirely from term-mode whiskering equalities and chain them with `.trans`.
564	  have h1 := CategoryTheory.unit_conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α c
565	  -- the two `homEquiv_unit` expansions, with all implicits fixed by the stated types.
566	  have huA : adj₂.homEquiv c d (α.app c ≫ f)
567	      = adj₂.unit.app c ≫ R₂.map (α.app c ≫ f) :=
568	    Adjunction.homEquiv_unit adj₂ c d (α.app c ≫ f)
569	  have huB : adj₁.homEquiv c d f = adj₁.unit.app c ≫ R₁.map f :=
570	    Adjunction.homEquiv_unit adj₁ c d f
571	  -- LHS transpose, in left-bracketed shape.
572	  have e1 : adj₂.homEquiv c d (α.app c ≫ f)
573	      = (adj₂.unit.app c ≫ R₂.map (α.app c)) ≫ R₂.map f :=
574	    huA.trans <| (CategoryTheory.whisker_eq (adj₂.unit.app c) (R₂.map_comp (α.app c) f)).trans
575	      (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm
576	  -- RHS transpose, in the same left-bracketed shape.
577	  have e2 : adj₁.homEquiv c d f ≫ (CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).app d
578	      = (adj₁.unit.app c ≫ (CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).app (L₁.obj c))
579	          ≫ R₂.map f :=
580	    (CategoryTheory.eq_whisker huB
581	        ((CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).app d)).trans <|
582	      (Category.assoc _ _ _).trans <|
583	        (CategoryTheory.whisker_eq (adj₁.unit.app c)
584	          ((CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).naturality f)).trans
585	          (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm
586	  exact e1.trans ((CategoryTheory.eq_whisker h1.symm (R₂.map f)).trans e2.symm)
587	
588	/-- **Unit coherence (`map_comp` keystone, `lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`).** The composite
589	analogue of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id`: the Mathlib free-pullback comparison at a composite
590	morphism `b ∘ a` factors through the pseudofunctor composition `pullbackComp`. Project-local.
591	
592	Transposing both sides under the composite pullback-pushforward adjunction: the LHS collapses
593	by `homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app` to `uTPU (b ≫ a) ≫ (conjugate of pullbackComp.hom)`, the
594	conjugate is `(pushforwardComp).inv` via `conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv` + `conjugateEquiv_comm`,
595	and the RHS collapses by `homEquiv` naturality to `uTPU a ≫ pushforward a (uTPU b)`;
596	both reduce to the unit-section identity (`pushforwardComp_inv_app_app = 𝟙`). -/
597	lemma pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp {Tx Ty Tz : Scheme.{u}} (a : Ty ⟶ Tx) (b : Tz ⟶ Ty) :
598	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app (SheafOfModules.unit Tx.ringCatSheaf) ≫
599	        SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (b ≫ a))

300	  TensorProduct.map LinearMap.id (actN S N)
301	
302	/-- Left action map `M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N) → M ⊗[ℤ] N`, `m ⊗ (s ⊗ n) ↦ (s • m) ⊗ n`. -/
303	noncomputable def actLmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
304	  (TensorProduct.map (actM S M) LinearMap.id).comp
305	    (TensorProduct.assoc ℤ M S N).symm.toLinearMap
306	
307	omit [Module S M] in
308	@[simp] lemma actRmap_tmul (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) :
309	    actRmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = m ⊗ₜ (s • n) := rfl
310	
311	omit [Module S N] in
312	@[simp] lemma actLmap_tmul (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) :
313	    actLmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = (s • m) ⊗ₜ n := rfl
314	
315	/-- The canonical projection `M ⊗[ℤ] N → M ⊗[S] N`, `m ⊗ n ↦ m ⊗ n`, as a `ℤ`-linear
316	map.  It is the cofork map exhibiting `M ⊗[S] N` as the coequalizer. -/
317	noncomputable def projL : (M ⊗[ℤ] N) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[S] N) :=
318	  (TensorProduct.liftAddHom
319	    { toFun := fun m =>
320	        (LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom (((TensorProduct.mk S M N) m).restrictScalars ℤ))
321	      map_zero' := by ext n; simp
322	      map_add' := fun m1 m2 => by ext n; simp }
323	    (fun r m n => by simp)).toIntLinearMap
324	
325	@[simp] lemma projL_tmul (m : M) (n : N) : projL S M N (m ⊗ₜ n) = m ⊗ₜ[S] n := rfl
326	
327	/-- The projection `M ⊗[ℤ] N → M ⊗[S] N` is surjective (it is the canonical
328	quotient map onto the relative tensor). -/
329	lemma projL_surjective : Function.Surjective (projL S M N) := by
330	  intro y
331	  induction y using TensorProduct.induction_on with
332	  | zero => exact ⟨0, map_zero _⟩
333	  | tmul m n => exact ⟨m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n, projL_tmul S M N m n⟩
334	  | add a b ha hb =>
335	    obtain ⟨pa, rfl⟩ := ha; obtain ⟨pb, rfl⟩ := hb; exact ⟨pa + pb, map_add _ _ _⟩
336	
337	/-- The two action maps become equal after the projection: this is the cofork
338	coequalizing condition, established at the level of `ℤ`-linear maps. -/
339	lemma projL_comp_act :
340	    (projL S M N).comp (actLmap S M N) = (projL S M N).comp (actRmap S M N) := by
341	  apply TensorProduct.ext'; intro m x
342	  induction x with
343	  | zero => rw [tmul_zero, map_zero, map_zero]
344	  | tmul s n =>
345	    change projL S M N (actLmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)))
346	      = projL S M N (actRmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)))
347	    rw [actLmap_tmul, actRmap_tmul, projL_tmul, projL_tmul, ← TensorProduct.smul_tmul',
348	      TensorProduct.tmul_smul]
349	  | add a b ha hb => rw [tmul_add, map_add, map_add, ha, hb]
350	
351	/-- Left action map as a morphism of abelian groups. -/
352	noncomputable def aL :
353	    AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) ⟶ AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
354	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (actLmap S M N).toAddMonoidHom
355	/-- Right action map as a morphism of abelian groups. -/
356	noncomputable def aR :
357	    AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) ⟶ AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
358	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (actRmap S M N).toAddMonoidHom
359	/-- The projection as a morphism of abelian groups. -/
360	noncomputable def piMor :
361	    AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] N) ⟶ AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[S] N) :=
362	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (projL S M N).toAddMonoidHom
363	
364	@[simp] lemma piMor_apply (x) : (ConcreteCategory.hom (piMor S M N)) x = projL S M N x := rfl
365	
366	instance piMor_epi : Epi (piMor S M N) :=
367	  ConcreteCategory.epi_of_surjective (piMor S M N) (projL_surjective S M N)
368	
369	/-- The projection coequalizes the two action maps (as morphisms of abelian groups). -/
370	lemma coeq_condition : aL S M N ≫ piMor S M N = aR S M N ≫ piMor S M N := by
371	  ext x; exact LinearMap.congr_fun (projL_comp_act S M N) x
372	
373	/-- The cofork `M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N) ⇉ M ⊗[ℤ] N → M ⊗[S] N` of abelian groups. -/
374	noncomputable def cofork : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N) :=
375	  Limits.Cofork.ofπ (piMor S M N) (coeq_condition S M N)
376	
377	/-- The descent map out of `M ⊗[S] N` induced by a cofork `s`: a pair of global
378	sections balanced under the `S`-action factors through the relative tensor.  This
379	is the universal property packaged by `TensorProduct.liftAddHom`. -/
380	noncomputable def descHom (s : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N)) :
381	    (M ⊗[S] N) →+ s.pt :=
382	  TensorProduct.liftAddHom
383	    { toFun := fun m =>
384	        { toFun := fun n => (ConcreteCategory.hom s.π) (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n)
385	          map_zero' := by rw [tmul_zero, map_zero]
386	          map_add' := fun n1 n2 => by rw [tmul_add, map_add] }
387	      map_zero' := by ext n; simp [zero_tmul]
388	      map_add' := fun m1 m2 => by ext n; simp [add_tmul] }
389	    (fun a m n => by
390	      simp only [AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
391	      have key :=
392	        congrArg (fun φ => (ConcreteCategory.hom φ) (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] (a ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n))) s.condition
393	      simpa using key)
394	
395	@[simp] lemma descHom_tmul (s : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N)) (m : M) (n : N) :
396	    descHom S M N s (m ⊗ₜ[S] n) = (ConcreteCategory.hom s.π) (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n) := rfl
397	
398	/-- The descent map as a morphism of abelian groups out of the cofork apex. -/
399	noncomputable def descMor (s : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N)) :
400	    (cofork S M N).pt ⟶ s.pt :=
401	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (descHom S M N s)
402	
403	/-- The descent map factors the cofork's projection: `π ≫ descMor s = s.π`. -/
404	lemma descFac (s : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N)) :
405	    (cofork S M N).π ≫ descMor S M N s = s.π := by
406	  ext x
407	  induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
408	  | zero => simp
409	  | tmul m n =>
410	    change descHom S M N s (projL S M N (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n)) = (ConcreteCategory.hom s.π) (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n)
411	    rw [projL_tmul, descHom_tmul]
412	  | add a b ha hb => simp only [map_add, ha, hb]
413	
414	/-- **`M ⊗[S] N` is the coequalizer**, in the category of abelian groups, of the two
415	`S`-action maps `M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N) ⇉ M ⊗[ℤ] N`.  This is the objectwise content of
416	`lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`; uniqueness uses that the projection `piMor` is an
417	epimorphism.  Axiom-clean. -/
418	noncomputable def isColimitCofork : Limits.IsColimit (cofork S M N) :=
419	  Limits.Cofork.IsColimit.mk _ (descMor S M N) (descFac S M N)
420	    (fun s _ hf => (cancel_epi (piMor S M N)).mp (hf.trans (descFac S M N s).symm))
421	
422	end RelativeTensorCoequalizer
423	
424	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — presheaf promotion of the coequalizer (Step 1)
425	
426	The objectwise coequalizer `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.isColimitCofork` exhibits, for a fixed
427	open `U`, the relative tensor `Γ(U,P) ⊗_{R(U)} Γ(U,Q)` as a coequalizer of the two
428	`R(U)`-action maps on `Γ(U,P) ⊗_ℤ R(U) ⊗_ℤ Γ(U,Q) ⇉ Γ(U,P) ⊗_ℤ Γ(U,Q)`.  To promote this
429	to the functor category `(Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab` (where colimits are computed objectwise, via
430	`CategoryTheory.Limits.evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits`) one first needs the two **domain
431	presheaves of the cofork as honest functors**, whose restriction maps are the `ℤ`-tensors of
432	the underlying restriction maps.  This section builds the first of those two functors
433	(`relTensorDomainPresheaf`, the `Γ(-,P) ⊗_ℤ Γ(-,Q)` presheaf); it is the concrete Step-1 brick
434	of `lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer` (`relativeTensorCoequalizerIso`).
435	
436	See the handoff note at the end of the file for the verified recipe for the remaining pieces
437	(triple-tensor presheaf, the natural action/projection transformations, the colimit lift, and
438	the apex identification) and the heartbeat/coercion friction points that must be budgeted. -/
439	
440	open scoped TensorProduct
441	
442	/-- The objectwise `ℤ`-tensor presheaf `U ↦ Γ(U,P) ⊗_ℤ Γ(U,Q)` of two presheaves of modules
443	over a scheme, as a functor into abelian groups, with restriction maps the `ℤ`-tensors of the
444	two underlying restriction maps.  This is the codomain (apex-adjacent) presheaf of the cofork
445	in the presheaf promotion of `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.isColimitCofork`; it is the concrete
446	Step-1 brick of the presheaf-level coequalizer iso `relativeTensorCoequalizerIso`

966	Nitsure §4 inductive step). For `A` a noetherian domain and `d ≥ 0`, a finite
967	module `N` over the polynomial ring `P_d := A[X_1,…,X_d]`, there exist `m ∈ ℕ`
968	and an injective `P_d`-linear map `φ : P_d^{⊕m} → N` whose cokernel
969	`T := N ⧸ range φ` is a torsion `P_d`-module. Equivalently, a short exact
970	sequence `0 → P_d^{⊕m} → N → T → 0` with `T` torsion.
971	
972	`m` is the generic rank: `dim_{Frac P_d} (Frac P_d ⊗_{P_d} N)`. The map `φ`
973	is the `P_d`-linear combination of `m` lifts `v : Fin m → N` whose images form a
974	`Frac P_d`-basis of the localisation `N_Q := LocalizedModule (P_d)⁰ N`. This step
975	is built over `P_d` directly — no inversion of any `g ∈ A` is required. -/

978	    (d : ℕ) (N : Type*) [AddCommGroup N]
979	    [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N]
980	    [Module A N] [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] :
981	    ∃ (m : ℕ) (φ : (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A)
982	        →ₗ[MvPolynomial (Fin d) A] N),
983	      Function.Injective φ ∧
984	      Module.IsTorsion (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ) := by
985	  classical

987	  -- localisation `NK = N_Q`, and the localisation map `ℓ : N → NK`.
988	  let P := MvPolynomial (Fin d) A
989	  let S := nonZeroDivisors P
990	  let K := Localization S
991	  let NK := LocalizedModule S N
992	  let ℓ : N →ₗ[P] NK := LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap S N
993	  -- `NK` is a finite-dimensional `K`-vector space; take a basis indexed by `Fin m`.
994	  let m := Module.finrank K NK

996	  -- Lift each basis vector along `ℓ`, clearing its denominator.

998	      ∃ lift : Fin m → N × S, ∀ i, ((lift i).2 : P) • b i = ℓ (lift i).1 := by
999	    refine ⟨fun i => (IsLocalizedModule.surj S ℓ (b i)).choose, fun i => ?_⟩

654	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp b a).inv.app (SheafOfModules.unit Tz.ringCatSheaf)

670	/-- **Free coherence (`map_comp`).** Composite analogue of `pullbackFreeIso_id`: the
671	free-pullback isomorphism at a composite `b ∘ a` factors through the pseudofunctor composition
672	`pullbackComp`. Reduces, by coproduct extensionality (`free = ∐ unit`), to the unit coherence
673	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`. Project-local. -/
674	lemma pullbackFreeIso_comp {Tx Ty Tz : Scheme.{u}} (a : Ty ⟶ Tx) (b : Tz ⟶ Ty) (I : Type u) :
675	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app (SheafOfModules.free (R := Tx.ringCatSheaf) I) ≫
676	        (pullbackFreeIso (b ≫ a) I).hom

678	        (pullbackFreeIso b I).hom := by
679	  haveI := opensMap_final (b ≫ a)
680	  haveI := opensMap_final a
681	  haveI := opensMap_final b
682	  refine Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext (isColimitCofanMkObjOfIsColimit
683	    (SheafOfModules.pullback (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a) ⋙
684	      SheafOfModules.pullback (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b)) _ _
685	    (SheafOfModules.isColimitFreeCofan (R := Tx.ringCatSheaf) I)) _ _ (fun i => ?_)
686	  simp only [cofan_mk_inj]

689	  -- `pullback_map_ιFree_comp_pullbackObjFreeIso_hom`, to `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` whiskered.
690	  -- the free-cofan comparison, restated in `pullbackFreeIso` form (defeq) so `congrArg` matches.
691	  -- each pullback changes the base ring sheaf: `Tx ↝ Ty ↝ Tz`.

693	          (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Tx.ringCatSheaf) i) ≫
694	        (pullbackFreeIso (b ≫ a) I).hom
695	      = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (b ≫ a)) ≫
696	        SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Tz.ringCatSheaf) i :=
697	    SheafOfModules.pullback_map_ιFree_comp_pullbackObjFreeIso_hom
698	      (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (b ≫ a)) (I := I) i
699	  have key_a : (Scheme.Modules.pullback a).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Tx.ringCatSheaf) i) ≫
700	        (pullbackFreeIso a I).hom
701	      = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a) ≫
702	        SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Ty.ringCatSheaf) i :=
703	    SheafOfModules.pullback_map_ιFree_comp_pullbackObjFreeIso_hom
704	      (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a) (I := I) i
705	  have key_b : (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Ty.ringCatSheaf) i) ≫
706	        (pullbackFreeIso b I).hom
707	      = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b) ≫
708	        SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Tz.ringCatSheaf) i :=
709	    SheafOfModules.pullback_map_ιFree_comp_pullbackObjFreeIso_hom
710	      (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b) (I := I) i
711	  -- LHS: naturality of `pullbackComp.hom` + free-cofan comparison at `b ≫ a`.
712	  have hLHS :
713	      (SheafOfModules.pullback (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a) ⋙
714	          SheafOfModules.pullback (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b)).map
715	            (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Tx.ringCatSheaf) i) ≫
716	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app (SheafOfModules.free (R := Tx.ringCatSheaf) I) ≫
717	          (pullbackFreeIso (b ≫ a) I).hom
718	      = ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app (SheafOfModules.unit Tx.ringCatSheaf) ≫
719	          SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (b ≫ a))) ≫
720	            (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Tz.ringCatSheaf) i) :=
721	    calc (SheafOfModules.pullback (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a) ⋙
722	            SheafOfModules.pullback (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b)).map
723	            (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Tx.ringCatSheaf) i) ≫
724	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app (SheafOfModules.free (R := Tx.ringCatSheaf) I) ≫
725	            (pullbackFreeIso (b ≫ a) I).hom
726	        = ((SheafOfModules.pullback (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a) ⋙
727	              SheafOfModules.pullback (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b)).map
728	              (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Tx.ringCatSheaf) i) ≫
729	            (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app

732	      _ = ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app (SheafOfModules.unit Tx.ringCatSheaf) ≫
733	            (Scheme.Modules.pullback (b ≫ a)).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Tx.ringCatSheaf) i)) ≫

736	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.naturality
737	              (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Tx.ringCatSheaf) i))
738	      _ = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app (SheafOfModules.unit Tx.ringCatSheaf) ≫

740	              (pullbackFreeIso (b ≫ a) I).hom := Category.assoc _ _ _

742	            SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (b ≫ a)) ≫
743	              (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Tz.ringCatSheaf) i) :=

745	            (SheafOfModules.unit Tx.ringCatSheaf) ≫ ·) key_ba

747	            SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (b ≫ a))) ≫
748	              (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Tz.ringCatSheaf) i) := (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm

752	          SheafOfModules.pullback (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b)).map
753	            (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Tx.ringCatSheaf) i) ≫
754	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map (pullbackFreeIso a I).hom

756	            (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a)) ≫

806	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian
807	
808	/-- The **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(r,d)` (`def:grassmannian_functor`): the
809	contravariant functor from schemes to sets sending `T` to the set of equivalence classes
810	of rank-`d` locally free quotients `q : O_T^r ↠ F`, acting on morphisms by pullback.
811	
812	The object and morphism assignments are complete; the functoriality laws (`map_id`,
813	`map_comp`) are discharged — via the naturality of the pseudofunctor comparison isomorphisms
814	`pullbackId`/`pullbackComp` of `Scheme.Modules.pullback` — through the free-sheaf coherences
815	`pullbackFreeIso_id`/`pullbackFreeIso_comp`, which reduce by coproduct extensionality to the
816	unit-level coherences `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id`/`pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`. Fully proved. -/
817	noncomputable def functor (d r : ℕ) : Scheme.{0}ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type 1 where
818	  obj T := Quotient (rqSetoid r d T.unop)
819	  map {X Y} g := TypeCat.ofHom (Quotient.map (rqPullback (r := r) (d := d) g.unop)
820	    (fun _ _ h => rqPullback_rel g.unop h))
821	  map_id X := by
822	    -- reduce to the equivalence relation on representatives
823	    ext z
824	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
825	    | _ x =>
826	      change Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback (𝟙 X).unop x) = Quotient.mk _ x
827	      -- the canonical iso `(𝟙)^* x.F ≅ x.F` is `pullbackId`; the quotient-map equation it
828	      -- must satisfy reduces, by naturality of `pullbackId.hom`, to the free coherence
829	      -- `pullbackFreeIso (𝟙) = (pullbackId).app (free _)`, hence to the unit-level identity
830	      -- `pullbackObjUnitToUnit (𝟙) = (pullbackId).app unit`. That coherence between
831	      -- `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` and Mathlib's `pullbackId` is the open obstacle.
832	      refine Quotient.sound ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X.unop).app x.F, ?_⟩
833	      -- unfold `(rqPullback (𝟙) x).q` and `(pullbackId.app x.F).hom` (defeq)
834	      change ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (𝟙 X.unop) (Fin r)).inv ≫
835	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 X.unop)).map x.q) ≫
836	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X.unop).hom.app x.F = x.q
837	      rw [Category.assoc, (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X.unop).hom.naturality x.q,
838	        ← Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_id]
839	      -- `(𝟭).map x.q = x.q` is only defeq, so close by term (rw can't see through it)
840	      exact Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc _ _
841	  map_comp {X Y Z} f g := by
842	    ext z
843	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
844	    | _ x =>
845	      change Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback (f ≫ g).unop x)
846	        = Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback g.unop (rqPullback f.unop x))
847	      -- the canonical iso `(g.unop ≫ f.unop)^* x.F ≅ g.unop^*(f.unop^* x.F)` is `pullbackComp`;
848	      -- the quotient-map equation reduces, by naturality, to the composite free coherence
849	      -- relating `pullbackFreeIso (g.unop ≫ f.unop)` to `pullbackFreeIso g.unop`/`f.unop`
850	      -- through `pullbackComp` — the composite analogue of the `map_id` obstacle.
851	      refine Quotient.sound ⟨((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).app x.F).symm, ?_⟩
852	      -- unfold `(rqPullback (g∘f) x).q` and `(pullbackComp.app x.F).symm.hom` (defeq), writing
853	      -- the composite as `g.unop ≫ f.unop` so the `pullbackComp` naturality matches syntactically
854	      change ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (g.unop ≫ f.unop) (Fin r)).inv ≫
855	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback (g.unop ≫ f.unop)).map x.q) ≫
856	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).inv.app x.F
857	        = (rqPullback g.unop (rqPullback f.unop x)).q
858	      -- expose the `pullbackComp.inv` naturality square (mirrors the `map_id` reduction)
859	      rw [Category.assoc, (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).inv.naturality x.q]
860	      -- the composite free coherence (`pullbackFreeIso_comp`) in inverse form: invert both
861	      -- sides of the iso equation `pullbackComp.hom.app free ≫ pfba.hom = (pullback g).map pfa.hom
862	      -- ≫ pfb.hom`.
863	      have hstar : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (g.unop ≫ f.unop) (Fin r)).inv ≫
864	            (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).inv.app (SheafOfModules.free (Fin r))
865	          = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso g.unop (Fin r)).inv ≫
866	            (Scheme.Modules.pullback g.unop).map
867	              (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso f.unop (Fin r)).inv := by
868	        have hH := Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_comp f.unop g.unop (Fin r)
869	        rw [← cancel_epi ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).hom.app
870	          (SheafOfModules.free (Fin r)) ≫
871	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (g.unop ≫ f.unop) (Fin r)).hom)]
872	        trans (𝟙 _)
873	        · rw [Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
874	          exact (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).hom_inv_id_app _
875	        · rw [hH]; simp
876	      -- whisker `hstar` by `≫ (pullback f ⋙ pullback g).map x.q` and refold the RHS via
877	      -- `map_comp` into `(rqPullback g (rqPullback f x)).q`.
878	      exact (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm.trans
879	        ((congrArg (· ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback f.unop ⋙
880	              Scheme.Modules.pullback g.unop).map x.q) hstar).trans
881	          ((Category.assoc _ _ _).trans
882	            (congrArg ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso g.unop (Fin r)).inv ≫ ·)
883	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g.unop).map_comp
884	                (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso f.unop (Fin r)).inv
885	                ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f.unop).map x.q)).symm)))
886	
887	/-- **`Gr(d,r)` represents the Grassmannian functor** (`thm:grassmannian_universal_property`):
888	the tautological quotient `⟨U, u⟩` exhibits `Gr(d,r)` as the fine moduli space of rank-`d`
889	quotients of `O^r`, i.e. `Hom(T, Gr(d,r)) ≅ Grass(r,d)(T)` naturally in `T`.
890	
891	NOTE: `functor` and `Scheme.Modules.glue` have landed; this rides on `tautologicalQuotient`
892	(hence on the bundle cocycle, the only remaining upstream gap). Body = the local-to-global
893	inverse construction of Nitsure §1 (a `T`-point of `Gr` is a rank-`d` quotient; conversely a
894	quotient determines, chart by chart, a map to each `U^I`, glued via the universal property of
895	the glued scheme). -/
896	noncomputable def represents (d r : ℕ) (hd : 1 ≤ d) (hdr : d ≤ r) :
897	    (functor d r).RepresentableBy (scheme d r) :=
898	  sorry
899	
900	end AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

1203	/-- Over a domain, the `T`-transform of a non-zero `f` has, in `X_0`, a leading
1204	coefficient equal to `C (coeff v f)` for some `v ∈ f.support` (hence non-zero). The
1205	Mathlib original concludes `IsUnit` using the field hypothesis; here we keep the exact
1206	coefficient so the caller can invert it. -/
1207	private lemma T_leadingcoeff_eq (fne : f ≠ 0) :
1208	    ∃ v ∈ f.support,
1209	      (finSuccEquiv k n (T f f)).leadingCoeff = MvPolynomial.C (coeff v f) := by
1210	  obtain ⟨v, vin, vs⟩ := Finset.exists_max_image f.support
1211	    (fun v ↦ (T f ((monomial v) (coeff v f))).degreeOf 0) (support_nonempty.mpr fne)
1212	  set h := fun w ↦ (MvPolynomial.monomial w) (coeff w f)
1213	  simp only [← natDegree_finSuccEquiv] at vs
1214	  replace vs : ∀ x ∈ f.support \ {v}, (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h x))).degree <
1215	      (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h v))).degree := by
1216	    intro x hx
1217	    obtain ⟨h1, h2⟩ := Finset.mem_sdiff.mp hx
1218	    apply degree_lt_degree <| lt_of_le_of_ne (vs x h1) ?_
1219	    simpa only [natDegree_finSuccEquiv]
1220	      using degreeOf_t_ne_of_ne f _ _ h1 vin <| ne_of_not_mem_cons h2

1222	      (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h v))).leadingCoeff := by
1223	    simp only [map_add, map_sum]
1224	    rw [add_comm]
1225	    apply leadingCoeff_add_of_degree_lt <| (lt_of_le_of_lt <| degree_sum_le _ _) ?_
1226	    have h2 : h v ≠ 0 := by simpa [h] using mem_support_iff.mp vin
1227	    replace h2 : (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h v))) ≠ 0 := fun eq ↦ h2 <|
1228	      by simpa only [map_eq_zero_iff _ (AlgEquiv.injective _)] using eq
1229	    exact (Finset.sup_lt_iff <| Ne.bot_lt (fun x ↦ h2 <| degree_eq_bot.mp x)).mpr vs
1230	  refine ⟨v, vin, ?_⟩
1231	  rw [leadingCoeff_finSuccEquiv_t] at coeff
1232	  nth_rw 2 [← f.support_sum_monomial_coeff]
1233	  rw [Finset.sum_eq_add_sum_diff_singleton_of_mem vin h, coeff, algebraMap_eq]

1235	/-- **L5b.2 — Nagata change of variables: monic in the distinguished variable**
1236	(`lem:gf_nagata_monic_lastVar`, Nitsure §4). For `A` a domain and `0 ≠ F ∈
1237	A[X_0,…,X_m]`, there is a triangular `A`-algebra automorphism `e` and a non-zero
1238	`g ∈ A` such that, after inverting `g`, the image of `e F` in `A_g[X_0,…,X_m]` —
1239	viewed via `finSuccEquiv` as a univariate polynomial in `X_0` — has a *unit* leading
1240	coefficient. (Variable convention: `finSuccEquiv` singles out `X_0`, not `X_m`; the
1241	choice is immaterial up to renaming.) -/
1242	theorem gf_nagata_monic_lastVar
1243	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] (m : ℕ)
1244	    (F : MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A) (hF : F ≠ 0) :
1245	    ∃ (g : A) (_ : g ≠ 0)
1246	      (e : MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A ≃ₐ[A] MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A),
1247	      IsUnit
1248	        (MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv (Localization.Away g) m
1249	          (MvPolynomial.map (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g)) (e F))).leadingCoeff := by
1250	  obtain ⟨v, vin, hlc⟩ := T_leadingcoeff_eq F hF
1251	  have hg0 : coeff v F ≠ 0 := mem_support_iff.mp vin
1252	  refine ⟨coeff v F, hg0, T F, ?_⟩
1253	  set g := coeff v F with hg
1254	  haveI : Nontrivial (Localization.Away g) := by
1255	    have hle : Submonoid.powers g ≤ nonZeroDivisors A := by
1256	      rw [Submonoid.powers_le]; exact mem_nonZeroDivisors_of_ne_zero hg0
1257	    exact (IsLocalization.injective (Localization.Away g) hle).nontrivial
1258	  have hgunit : IsUnit (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) g) :=
1259	    IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit g
1260	  have hCunit : IsUnit (MvPolynomial.C (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) g)
1261	      : MvPolynomial (Fin m) (Localization.Away g)) := hgunit.map MvPolynomial.C
1262	  have hne : (MvPolynomial.map (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g)))
1263	      (finSuccEquiv A m (T F F)).leadingCoeff ≠ 0 := by
1264	    rw [hlc, MvPolynomial.map_C]; exact hCunit.ne_zero
1265	  rw [finSuccEquiv_map_comm, leadingCoeff_map_of_leadingCoeff_ne_zero _ hne, hlc,
1266	    MvPolynomial.map_C]
1267	  exact hCunit
1268

1272	`rename Fin.succ`: the composite `S = R[X_1,…,X_n] → R[X_0,…,X_n] → S[X_0]` is the

1274	`optionEquivLeft` variant (`finSuccEquiv_rename_finSuccEquiv`). -/
1275	private theorem finSuccEquiv_rename_succ
1276	    (R : Type*) [CommRing R] (n : ℕ) (s : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) :
1277	    MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n (MvPolynomial.rename Fin.succ s) = Polynomial.C s := by

1279	  | C r => rw [MvPolynomial.rename_C, MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv_apply, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]; rfl
1280	  | add p q hp hq => simp [hp, hq]

1282	    rw [map_mul, map_mul, hp, MvPolynomial.rename_X, MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv_X_succ,
1283	      Polynomial.C_mul]

1286	(`lem:gf_mvPolynomial_quotient_finite_monic`, Nitsure §4). Let `R` be a commutative
1287	ring and `p ∈ R[X_0,…,X_n]` a polynomial whose image under `finSuccEquiv` (a
1288	univariate polynomial in `X_0` over `S := R[X_1,…,X_n]`) has a *unit* leading
1289	coefficient — i.e. `p` is monic in `X_0` up to a unit. Then `R[X_0,…,X_n]/(p)` is
1290	module-finite over `S = R[X_1,…,X_n]`, the structure being via the constant
1291	inclusion `S ↪ R[X_0,…,X_n]` (`rename Fin.succ`) followed by `Ideal.Quotient.mk`.
1292	
1293	Encoded as `RingHom.Finite` of that composite ring map (which unfolds to
1294	`Module.Finite S (R[X_0,…,X_n]/(p))` with the exotic algebra structure) to avoid an
1295	in-statement `letI` whose `Module` synthesis loops. The proof rescales `finSuccEquiv p`

1297	same ideal, invokes `Polynomial.Monic.finite_quotient`, and transports along the
1298	`S`-algebra isomorphism induced by `finSuccEquiv`. -/
1299	theorem mvPolynomial_quotient_finite_of_monic_lastVar
1300	    (R : Type*) [CommRing R] (n : ℕ)
1301	    (p : MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R)
1302	    (hp : IsUnit (MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p).leadingCoeff) :
1303	    ((Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {p})).comp
1304	        (MvPolynomial.rename Fin.succ).toRingHom :
1305	          MvPolynomial (Fin n) R →+* (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p})).Finite := by
1306	  letI algI : Algebra (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p}) :=
1307	    ((Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {p})).comp
1308	      (MvPolynomial.rename Fin.succ).toRingHom).toAlgebra
1309	  have hc : IsUnit ((hp.unit⁻¹ : (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)ˣ) : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) :=
1310	    (hp.unit⁻¹).isUnit
1311	  have hcP :
1312	      IsUnit (Polynomial.C ((hp.unit⁻¹ : (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)ˣ) : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)) :=
1313	    hc.map Polynomial.C
1314	  have hmonic : (Polynomial.C ((hp.unit⁻¹ : (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)ˣ) : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)
1315	      * MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p).Monic := by
1316	    rw [Polynomial.Monic, Polynomial.leadingCoeff_C_mul_of_isUnit hc]
1317	    exact Units.inv_mul_of_eq hp.unit_spec
1318	  have hspan : Ideal.span {Polynomial.C ((hp.unit⁻¹ : (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)ˣ) :
1319	        MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) * MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p}

1321	    Ideal.span_singleton_mul_left_unit hcP (MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p)

1354	/-- Pull back an `R`-module structure along an additive equivalence `e : M ≃+ N`, defining
1355	`r • y := e (r • e.symm y)`. With this structure `e` is `R`-linear; used in the torsion
1356	reindex to transport the reindexed module structure from the `P`-localisation of the torsion
1357	module to the goal's `A`-localisation. -/
1358	@[reducible] def pullbackModuleAddEquiv {R M N : Type*} [Semiring R] [AddCommMonoid M]
1359	    [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R M] (e : M ≃+ N) : Module R N where
1360	  smul r y := e (r • e.symm y)
1361	  one_smul y := by change e (1 • e.symm y) = y; rw [one_smul, AddEquiv.apply_symm_apply]
1362	  mul_smul r s y := by
1363	    change e ((r * s) • e.symm y) = e (r • e.symm (e (s • e.symm y)))
1364	    rw [AddEquiv.symm_apply_apply, mul_smul]
1365	  smul_zero r := by change e (r • e.symm 0) = 0; rw [map_zero, smul_zero, map_zero]
1366	  smul_add r x y := by
1367	    change e (r • e.symm (x + y)) = e (r • e.symm x) + e (r • e.symm y)
1368	    rw [map_add, smul_add, map_add]
1369	  add_smul r s y := by
1370	    change e ((r + s) • e.symm y) = e (r • e.symm y) + e (s • e.symm y)
1371	    rw [add_smul, map_add]
1372	  zero_smul y := by change e (0 • e.symm y) = 0; rw [zero_smul, map_zero]
1373	
1374	/-- Module-finiteness transports across the pulled-back structure of `pullbackModuleAddEquiv`:
1375	if `M` is a finite `R`-module then so is `N` under the pulled-back action. -/
1376	theorem finite_of_pullbackModuleAddEquiv {R M N : Type*} [Semiring R] [AddCommMonoid M]
1377	    [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R M] [Module.Finite R M] (e : M ≃+ N) :
1378	    @Module.Finite R N _ _ (pullbackModuleAddEquiv e) := by
1379	  letI : Module R N := pullbackModuleAddEquiv e
1380	  refine Module.Finite.equiv (M := M) (N := N)
1381	    { toFun := e, map_add' := map_add e, invFun := e.symm,
1382	      left_inv := e.left_inv, right_inv := e.right_inv,
1383	      map_smul' := fun r x => ?_ }
1384	  simp only [RingHom.id_apply]
1385	  change e (r • x) = e (r • e.symm (e x))
1386	  rw [AddEquiv.symm_apply_apply]
1387	
1388	/-- A scalar tower transports across the pulled-back structures of `pullbackModuleAddEquiv`:
1389	if `e : M ≃+ N` and `M` carries compatible `Ag`- and `R`-actions (`IsScalarTower Ag R M`), then
1390	the pulled-back `Ag`- and `R`-actions on `N` form a scalar tower as well. -/
1391	theorem pullback_isScalarTower {Ag R M N : Type*} [CommSemiring Ag] [Semiring R] [Algebra Ag R]
1392	    [AddCommMonoid M] [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R M] [Module Ag M] [IsScalarTower Ag R M]
1393	    (e : M ≃+ N) :
1394	    letI := pullbackModuleAddEquiv (R := R) e
1395	    letI := pullbackModuleAddEquiv (R := Ag) e
1396	    IsScalarTower Ag R N := by
1397	  letI := pullbackModuleAddEquiv (R := R) e
1398	  letI := pullbackModuleAddEquiv (R := Ag) e
1399	  refine ⟨fun a b x => ?_⟩
1400	  change e ((a • b) • e.symm x) = e (a • e.symm (e (b • e.symm x)))
1401	  rw [AddEquiv.symm_apply_apply, smul_assoc]
1402	
1403	/-- Transport module-finiteness across a ring isomorphism of the acting ring that is
1404	compatible with given `R`-algebra structures. If `ψ : B₁ ≃+* B₂` satisfies
1405	`ψ ∘ algebraMap R B₁ = algebraMap R B₂`, `B₂` is module-finite over `R`, and `M` is
1406	module-finite over `B₁` (with the scalar tower `R → B₁ → M`), then `M` is module-finite
1407	over `R`. -/
1408	theorem finite_of_quotientRingEquiv
1409	    {R B₁ B₂ M : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing B₁] [CommRing B₂] [AddCommGroup M]
1410	    [Algebra R B₁] [Algebra R B₂] [Module B₁ M] [Module R M] [IsScalarTower R B₁ M]
1411	    (ψ : B₁ ≃+* B₂)
1412	    (hψ : ∀ r : R, ψ (algebraMap R B₁ r) = algebraMap R B₂ r)
1413	    (hB₂ : Module.Finite R B₂) (hM : Module.Finite B₁ M) :
1414	    Module.Finite R M := by
1415	  haveI := hB₂
1416	  haveI := hM
1417	  have hsymm : ∀ r : R, ψ.symm (algebraMap R B₂ r) = algebraMap R B₁ r := by
1418	    intro r; rw [← hψ r, ψ.symm_apply_apply]
1419	  haveI : Module.Finite R B₁ :=
1420	    Module.Finite.equiv (AlgEquiv.ofRingEquiv (f := ψ.symm) hsymm).toLinearEquiv
1421	  exact Module.Finite.trans B₁ M

1423	/-- Descent of a localized-module structure along a scalar tower. If `f : M →ₗ[R] M'`
1424	localises `M` at the image submonoid `S.map (algebraMap R' R)` over `R`, then its
1425	restriction of scalars to `R'` localises `M` at `S` over `R'`. -/
1426	theorem isLocalizedModule_restrictScalars
1427	    {R' R M M' : Type*} [CommRing R'] [CommRing R] [Algebra R' R]
1428	    [AddCommGroup M] [AddCommGroup M'] [Module R' M] [Module R M] [Module R' M'] [Module R M']
1429	    [IsScalarTower R' R M] [IsScalarTower R' R M']
1430	    (S : Submonoid R') (f : M →ₗ[R] M')
1431	    [IsLocalizedModule (S.map (algebraMap R' R)) f] :
1432	    IsLocalizedModule S (f.restrictScalars R') := by
1433	  apply IsLocalizedModule.mk
1434	  · intro s
1435	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff]
1436	    have hmem : algebraMap R' R (s : R') ∈ S.map (algebraMap R' R) := ⟨s, s.2, rfl⟩
1437	    have hu := IsLocalizedModule.map_units f ⟨algebraMap R' R (s : R'), hmem⟩
1438	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff] at hu
1439	    have hfun : ⇑((algebraMap R' (Module.End R' M')) (s : R'))
1440	        = ⇑((algebraMap R (Module.End R M')) (algebraMap R' R (s : R'))) := by
1441	      funext x
1442	      rw [Module.algebraMap_end_apply, Module.algebraMap_end_apply, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul]
1443	    rw [hfun]
1444	    exact hu
1445	  · intro y
1446	    obtain ⟨⟨t, sm⟩, hsm⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj (S.map (algebraMap R' R)) f y
1447	    obtain ⟨s, hsS, hs⟩ := sm.2
1448	    refine ⟨⟨t, ⟨s, hsS⟩⟩, ?_⟩
1449	    change (s : R') • y = (f.restrictScalars R') t
1450	    rw [LinearMap.restrictScalars_apply, ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul R (s : R') y, hs]
1451	    exact hsm
1452	  · intro x₁ x₂ h
1453	    rw [LinearMap.restrictScalars_apply, LinearMap.restrictScalars_apply] at h
1454	    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq (S := S.map (algebraMap R' R)) (f := f) h
1455	    obtain ⟨s, hsS, hs⟩ := c.2
1456	    refine ⟨⟨s, hsS⟩, ?_⟩
1457	    change (s : R') • x₁ = (s : R') • x₂
1458	    rw [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul R (s : R') x₁,
1459	        ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul R (s : R') x₂, hs]
1460	    exact hc

1466	-- Elaboration of the verified `Module.Finite Qf Tg'` localisation chain plus the `A_g`-linearity
1467	-- transport for the final reindex is heavy.
1468	/-- **L5b — torsion reindex onto fewer variables** (`lem:gf_torsion_reindex`,
1469	Nitsure §4 support-dimension drop). For `A` a noetherian domain, `d ≥ 1`, and `T`
1470	a finite torsion module over `P_d := A[X_1,…,X_d]`, there exist `g ≠ 0` in `A` and
1471	`m' < d` such that, after inverting `g`, the localisation `T_g` is module-finite

1473	theorem gf_torsion_reindex

1704	/-- **Descent of generic freeness across a tower of `Away` localisations.** If after

1711	`(T_g)_h := LocalizedModule (powers h) (LocalizedModule (powers g) T)` over `(A_g)_h`. Here

1713	iterated localisation is the single localisation of `T` at `powers (g·a)`; transporting
1714	freeness across the ring iso (`Module.Free.of_ringEquiv`) and the localised-module
1715	identification gives `T_f` free over `A_f` with `f := g·a`. -/
1716	theorem free_localizationAway_of_away_tower
1717	    (A T : Type u) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [AddCommGroup T] [Module A T]
1718	    {g : A} (hg : g ≠ 0) {h : Localization.Away g} (hh : h ≠ 0)
1719	    (hfree : Module.Free (Localization.Away h)
1720	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T))) :
1721	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧

1728	  have hsunit : IsUnit (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) (s : A)) :=
1729	    IsLocalization.map_units (Localization.Away g) s
1730	  -- `algebraMap ↑s` is the unit `u`; record `algebraMap ↑s * u⁻¹ = 1`.
1731	  have hsu : algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) (s : A) * ↑(hsunit.unit⁻¹) = 1 := by
1732	    have h1 := hsunit.unit.mul_inv

1826	Nitsure §4, the genuine Mathlib-absent residue). For `A` a noetherian domain and
1827	`d ≥ 0`, a finite module `N` over the polynomial ring `A[X_1,…,X_d]`, regarded as

1832	finite-`A`-module leaf `exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`
1833	(`MvPolynomial (Fin 0) A ≅ A`). The inductive step builds the generic-rank short

1837	`exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact`. -/
1838	theorem exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial
1839	    (A : Type u) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
1840	    (d : ℕ) (N : Type u) [AddCommGroup N]
1841	    [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N]
1842	    [Module A N] [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] :
1843	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
1844	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) N) := by
1845	  -- The genuine proof is a *strong* induction on `d`, with the module `N` and its
1846	  -- instances universally quantified in the motive: the inductive hypothesis must
1847	  -- apply to the lower-support-dimension torsion quotient `T` (a module over a
1848	  -- polynomial ring in fewer variables `m < d`), and the module instances depend
1849	  -- on `d`, so a faithful induction must re-quantify `N` over each `d`. The
1850	  -- `generalizing N` reverts `N` together with all five of its `d`-dependent
1851	  -- instances, so the IH has the shape
1852	  --   IH : ∀ m < d, ∀ (N : Type _) [AddCommGroup N] [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin m) A) N]
1853	  --          [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin m) A) N] [Module A N]
1854	  --          [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin m) A) N],
1855	  --          ∃ f ≠ 0, Module.Free (A_f) (N_f).
1856	  induction d using Nat.strong_induction_on generalizing A N with
1857	  | _ d IH =>
1858	    rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos d with hd | hd
1859	    · -- Base case `d = 0`: `MvPolynomial (Fin 0) A ≅ A`, so `N` is module-finite
1860	      -- over `A` itself (via `Module.Finite.trans` through the iso), and the claim
1861	      -- is the finite-module leaf `exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`.
1862	      subst hd
1863	      haveI : Module.Finite A (MvPolynomial (Fin 0) A) :=
1864	        Module.Finite.equiv (MvPolynomial.isEmptyAlgEquiv A (Fin 0)).symm.toLinearEquiv
1865	      haveI : Module.Finite A N := Module.Finite.trans (MvPolynomial (Fin 0) A) N
1866	      exact exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A N
1867	    · -- Inductive step. Pass to `K = Frac A` and split on whether `N_K = 0`.
1868	      by_cases htors : Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors A) N)
1869	      · -- Torsion sub-case `N_K = 0`: closed by the L1 torsion base case applied
1870	        -- with the finite-type polynomial ring `B := A[X_1,…,X_d]` as the module's
1871	        -- ring of definition.

1873	      · -- Generic-rank dévissage (the genuine generic-rank residue): with `N_K ≠ 0`,

1876	        -- gives, after inverting some `g ≠ 0`, a short exact sequence
1877	        --   `0 → A_g[X]^{⊕m} → N_g → T → 0`
1878	        -- with `T` torsion of support dimension `< d`. After a Noether-normalisation
1879	        -- reindex `T` is finite over a polynomial ring in `m' < d` variables, so the
1880	        -- IH applies:  `IH m' (by …) T` yields `h ≠ 0` with `T_h` free over `A_h`.
1881	        -- The free middle term `A_g[X]^{⊕m}` localises to a free `A_{gh}`-module by
1882	        -- the `d = 0` leaf (`exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`) applied
1883	        -- coordinatewise, and `exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact` (L3)
1884	        -- splices the localised SES to give `N_f` free over `A_f` with `f := g·h`.
1885	        --
1886	        -- The IH is now genuinely in scope and quantifies over the base domain `A`
1887	        -- (the structural fix of this iter): `IH m' (hm' : m' < d) A_g T_g` typechecks
1888	        -- at the reindexed base `A_g = Localization.Away g`.
1889	        --
1890	        -- The generic-rank SES `0 → (Fin m → P) → N → T → 0` (`gf_generic_rank_ses`),

1893	        -- Step 1: the torsion cokernel `N ⧸ range φ` is a finite `P`-module (its restricted
1894	        -- `A`-module structure + scalar tower `A → P → quotient` are found by instances).
1895	        haveI hTfin : Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ) :=
1896	          Module.Finite.of_surjective (LinearMap.range φ).mkQ (LinearMap.range φ).mkQ_surjective
1897	        -- Step 2: reindex the torsion cokernel onto `m' < d` variables over `A_g`.
1898	        -- `gf_torsion_reindex` now emits its `MvPolynomial (Fin m') A_g`-action (`hmod1`) and the
1899	        -- scalar tower (`htower`) over the *canonical* (`inferInstance`) `Module A_g`-action on the
1900	        -- localised module — the redundant `Module A_g T_g` existential was dropped, so the
1901	        -- `A_g`-action the IH and `free_localizationAway_of_away_tower` synthesise is exactly the

1904	        obtain ⟨g, hg0, m', hm'lt, hmod1, htower, hfin⟩ :=
1905	          gf_torsion_reindex A d hd (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ) hTtors
1906	        -- Step 3: the inductive hypothesis at the new noetherian-domain base `A_g`.

1913	        letI := hmod1
1914	        haveI := hfin
1915	        haveI := htower
1916	        -- Steps 3–4: the IH at base `A_g` produces `hh ≠ 0` in `A_g` with the doubly-localised

1920	            ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧ Module.Free (Localization.Away f)
1921	              (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ)) := by
1922	          obtain ⟨hh, hh0, hfree_Tgh⟩ :=

1931	            (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A)) :=
1932	          (IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers f) (Localization.Away f)
1933	            (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f)
1934	              (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A))).free
1935	        exact exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A)
1936	          (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ) φ
1937	          (LinearMap.range φ).mkQ hφinj (LinearMap.range φ).mkQ_surjective
1938	          (by rw [LinearMap.exact_iff, Submodule.ker_mkQ]) hf0 hf0 hM'_free hTf_free

2068	            (B ⧸ p.asIdeal) (B ⧸ p.asIdeal) htors

2092	              (MvPolynomial (Fin n) (Localization.Away g)) Cg :=
2093	            IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun r => (φ.commutes r).symm

2096	            GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial (Localization.Away g) n Cg

2100	          -- with it, upgrading the `A`-linear bridge to an `A_g`-linear one.

2102	            IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun x => (hcompat x).symm
2103	          let e0 : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) C ≃ₗ[A] Cg :=
2104	            IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers g)
2105	              (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom A C Cg).toLinearMap
2106	          let e : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) C ≃ₗ[Localization.Away g] Cg :=
2107	            LinearEquiv.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization (Submonoid.powers g)

2113	            haveI := hCgfree
2114	            exact Module.Free.of_equiv
2115	              (IsLocalizedModule.mapEquiv (Submonoid.powers h)

2117	                (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers h)

2144	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — G1 affine-local finiteness (Stacks 01PB)
2145	
2146	The geometric form of generic flatness (`genericFlatness`) consumes the
2147	affine-local finiteness of the section module of a finite-type quasi-coherent
2148	sheaf (blueprint G1, `lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections`, Stacks Tag 01PB).
2149	This section builds the **locality reduction** for that statement: given a finite
2150	basic-open cover of an affine open `W` on each member of which the section module
2151	is module-finite, the section module on `W` is module-finite. It is assembled from
2152	the QUOT keystone `Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen` (gap2: the section
2153	restriction `Γ(F,W) → Γ(F, D(g))` is `IsLocalizedModule (powers g)`) together with
2154	Mathlib's finite-generation gluing `Module.Finite.of_localizationSpan_finite`.

2156	The remaining (genuinely geometric, Mathlib-absent) ingredient toward a complete
2157	G1 is the *base case*: on an affine where the finite-type generating sections of
2158	`F` are global, the section module is finite — i.e. the sheaf epimorphism
2159	`free I → F|_{D(g)}` (with `I` finite) is module-level surjective on global affine
2160	sections. That bridge (sheaf-epi ⟹ stalkwise surjective ⟹ module generation) is
2161	flagged in the file `task_results` handoff. -/

2163	/-- **Transfer of module finiteness across localized-module models.** If `Rₚ`
2164	localizes `R` at `S` and `φ : M →ₗ[R] N` exhibits `N` as the localization of `M`
2165	at `S` (with `N` an `Rₚ`-module compatibly), then finiteness of `N` over `Rₚ`
2166	transfers to finiteness of the *canonical* localized module `LocalizedModule S M`
2167	over `Localization S`. Used to feed the geometric section module `Γ(F, D(g))`
2168	(localized via the gap2 keystone over `Γ(X, D(g))`) into Mathlib's
2169	`Module.Finite.of_localizationSpan_finite`, whose per-element hypothesis is phrased
2170	in the canonical `Localization.Away`/`LocalizedModule.Away` models. Project-local:
2171	Mathlib has the canonical-model finiteness instance (needing global finiteness) but
2172	no model-independent transfer. -/
2173	theorem finite_localizedModule_of_isLocalizedModule
2174	    {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (S : Submonoid R) {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
2175	    {Rₚ : Type*} [CommRing Rₚ] [Algebra R Rₚ] [IsLocalization S Rₚ]
2176	    {N : Type*} [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [Module Rₚ N] [IsScalarTower R Rₚ N]
2177	    (φ : M →ₗ[R] N) [IsLocalizedModule S φ] [Module.Finite Rₚ N] :
2178	    Module.Finite (Localization S) (LocalizedModule S M) := by
2179	  classical
2180	  let e : N ≃ₗ[R] LocalizedModule S M :=
2181	    IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv S φ (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap S M)
2182	  let ψ : Rₚ ≃ₐ[R] Localization S := IsLocalization.algEquiv S Rₚ (Localization S)
2183	  have hsemi : ∀ (a : Rₚ) (x : N), e (a • x) = ψ a • e x := by
2184	    intro a x
2185	    obtain ⟨⟨r, s⟩, hrs⟩ := IsLocalization.mk'_surjective S a
2186	    have hsa : (s : R) • a = (algebraMap R Rₚ) r := by
2187	      rw [Algebra.smul_def, ← hrs, mul_comm, IsLocalization.mk'_spec]
2188	    have hunit : IsUnit ((algebraMap R (Module.End R (LocalizedModule S M))) (s : R)) :=
2189	      IsLocalizedModule.map_units (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap S M) s
2190	    have hbij := (Module.End.isUnit_iff _).mp hunit
2191	    apply hbij.injective
2192	    change (algebraMap R (Module.End R (LocalizedModule S M)) (s : R)) (e (a • x))
2193	       = (algebraMap R (Module.End R (LocalizedModule S M)) (s : R)) (ψ a • e x)
2194	    simp only [Module.algebraMap_end_apply]
2195	    rw [← map_smul e ((s:R)) (a • x)]
2196	    have hL : (s:R) • (a • x) = (r:R) • x := by
2197	      rw [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul Rₚ (s:R) (a • x), smul_smul, ← Algebra.smul_def, hsa,
2198	        IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul]
2199	    have hR : (s:R) • (ψ a • e x) = (r:R) • e x := by
2200	      rw [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul (Localization S) (s:R) (ψ a • e x), smul_smul,
2201	        ← Algebra.smul_def, ← map_smul ψ, hsa, ψ.commutes, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul]
2202	    rw [hL, map_smul e, hR]
2203	  obtain ⟨T, hT⟩ := (Module.Finite.fg_top (R := Rₚ) (M := N))
2204	  refine ⟨⟨T.image (e : N → LocalizedModule S M), ?_⟩⟩
2205	  rw [Finset.coe_image, eq_top_iff]
2206	  suffices h : ∀ z ∈ Submodule.span Rₚ (T : Set N),
2207	      e z ∈ Submodule.span (Localization S) (e '' (T : Set N)) by
2208	    intro y _
2209	    have := h (e.symm y) (by rw [hT]; trivial)
2210	    rwa [e.apply_symm_apply] at this
2211	  intro z hz
2212	  induction hz using Submodule.span_induction with
2213	  | mem z hz => exact Submodule.subset_span ⟨z, hz, rfl⟩
2214	  | zero => simp
2215	  | add a b _ _ ha hb => rw [map_add]; exact Submodule.add_mem _ ha hb
2216	  | smul a b _ hb => rw [hsemi]; exact Submodule.smul_mem _ _ hb

2218	/-- **G1 locality reduction (Stacks 01PB, geometric half).** Let `F` be a
2219	quasi-coherent sheaf of modules on a scheme `X` and `W` an affine open. If a finite
2220	family `t ⊆ Γ(X, W)` generates the unit ideal (so the basic opens `D(g)`, `g ∈ t`,
2221	cover `W`) and on each `D(g)` the section module `Γ(F, D(g))` is finite over

2223	
2224	Assembles the gap2 keystone `Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen` (section
2225	restriction to `D(g)` is `IsLocalizedModule (powers g)`) — with `Γ(X, D(g))` the

2231	theorem gf_finite_sections_of_basicOpen_finite_cover
2232	    {X : Scheme.{u}} (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
2233	    {W : X.Opens} (hW : IsAffineOpen W)
2234	    (t : Finset Γ(X, W)) (ht : Ideal.span (t : Set Γ(X, W)) = ⊤)
2235	    (H : ∀ g ∈ t, Module.Finite Γ(X, X.basicOpen g) Γ(F, X.basicOpen g)) :
2236	    Module.Finite Γ(X, W) Γ(F, W) := by
2237	  apply Module.Finite.of_localizationSpan_finite t ht
2238	  intro g
2239	  letI : Module Γ(X, W) Γ(F, X.basicOpen g.val) :=
2240	    Module.compHom _ (algebraMap Γ(X, W) Γ(X, X.basicOpen g.val))
2241	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(X, W) Γ(X, X.basicOpen g.val) Γ(F, X.basicOpen g.val) :=
2242	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul (fun _ _ => rfl)

2244	    hW.isLocalization_basicOpen g.val
2245	  haveI := Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen F hW g.val
2246	  haveI : Module.Finite Γ(X, X.basicOpen g.val) Γ(F, X.basicOpen g.val) := H g.val g.2
2247	  exact finite_localizedModule_of_isLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g.val)
2248	    (Rₚ := Γ(X, X.basicOpen g.val)) (Scheme.Modules.restrictBasicOpenₗ F g.val)

2250	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — G1 base case via the affine tilde–Γ adjunction
2251	
2252	The base case of blueprint G1 (`lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections`, Stacks 01PB):
2253	on an affine `V ≅ Spec R`, a quasi-coherent sheaf globally generated by finitely
2254	many sections has a finite section module. The crux is **seam 2**
2255	(`gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi`): the affine global-sections functor `moduleSpecΓFunctor`

2261	/-- **Seam 2 — `Γ` on affine quasi-coherent modules sends epis to surjections.**
2262	For `G, F : (Spec R).Modules` with both tilde–Γ counits `IsIso` (the quasi-coherent
2263	hypothesis, supplied by `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict` /
2264	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`) and `π : G ⟶ F` an epimorphism of sheaves of
2265	modules, the affine global-sections map `(moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π).hom` is a
2266	surjective `R`-linear map.
2267	
2268	Mechanism (no cohomology): the counit `fromTildeΓNatTrans` is natural in `π`, so
2269	`~(Γπ) = G.fromTildeΓ ≫ π ≫ inv F.fromTildeΓ` — a composite of an iso, the epi `π`,
2270	and an iso, hence an epi. `tilde.functor R` is faithful, so it reflects epimorphisms;
2271	therefore `Γπ` is epi in `ModuleCat R`, i.e. surjective. Project-local: Mathlib's
2272	`tilde.adjunction` supplies every ingredient but not this packaged corollary. -/
2273	theorem gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {G F : (Spec R).Modules}
2274	    (π : G ⟶ F) [Epi π] [IsIso G.fromTildeΓ] [IsIso F.fromTildeΓ] :
2275	    Function.Surjective (moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π).hom := by
2276	  rw [← ModuleCat.epi_iff_surjective]
2277	  refine (tilde.functor R).epi_of_epi_map ?_

2279	  simp only [Functor.comp_map, Functor.id_map] at hnat
2280	  change (tilde.functor R).map (moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π) ≫ F.fromTildeΓ
2281	       = G.fromTildeΓ ≫ π at hnat
2282	  have h : (tilde.functor R).map (moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π)
2283	         = (G.fromTildeΓ ≫ π) ≫ inv F.fromTildeΓ :=
2284	    (IsIso.eq_comp_inv F.fromTildeΓ).mpr hnat
2285	  rw [h]
2286	  haveI : Epi (inv F.fromTildeΓ) := inferInstance
2287	  exact epi_comp (G.fromTildeΓ ≫ π) (inv F.fromTildeΓ)

2301	    [IsIso G.fromTildeΓ] [IsIso F.fromTildeΓ]
2302	    [Module.Finite R (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj G)] :
2303	    Module.Finite R (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F) :=
2304	  Module.Finite.of_surjective (moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π).hom (gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi π)

2332	The seam-1 declarations of blueprint Half 2 (Stacks Tag 01PB): from the abstract
2333	finite-type local-generation datum of a quasi-coherent sheaf, extract a *standard*

2344	
2345	/-- **Seam 1b — finite standard basic-open subcover of an affine open.** Given an
2346	affine open `W` of a scheme `X` and an arbitrary open cover `{U i}` of `W`
2347	(`W ≤ ⨆ i, U i`), there is a finite family `t ⊆ Γ(X, W)` with
2348	`Ideal.span t = ⊤` (so the basic opens `D(g)`, `g ∈ t`, cover `W`) such that each
2349	`D(g)` is contained in some cover member `U i`. Mirrors the affine-communication
2350	template `AlgebraicGeometry.of_affine_open_cover`: each point of `W` sits in a basic
2351	open `D(f) ≤ U i` by `IsAffineOpen.exists_basicOpen_le`, the basic opens span by
2352	`IsAffineOpen.self_le_iSup_basicOpen_iff`, and quasi-compactness (packaged as
2353	`Ideal.span_eq_top_iff_finite`) extracts a finite subfamily. Project-local: the
2354	refinement-with-containment is not packaged in Mathlib. -/

2356	    {X : Scheme.{u}} {W : X.Opens} (hW : IsAffineOpen W)

2371	    intro x hx

2376	  intro g hg
