2406	
2407	`GeneratingSections.map` is the `GeneratingSections` analogue of Mathlib's
2408	`SheafOfModules.Presentation.mapGenerators`/`Presentation.map`. Given a
2409	colimit-preserving functor `F : SheafOfModules R ⥤ SheafOfModules S` together with a
2410	unit-iso `F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S`, a (finite) generating family of `M` transports to a
2411	(finite) generating family of `F.obj M` with the *same* index type. This is the engine of
2412	seam 1a (`gf_localGenerators_restrict`): restriction of a sheaf of modules along an open
2413	immersion is such a functor (via the project's slice-to-geometric equivalence

2416	`Presentation`s (kernel data included), not for bare generating families. -/

2420	universe v₁ u₁ v₂ u₂
2421	
2422	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}}
2423	  [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{u}] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
2424	  [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
2425	  {C' : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} C'] {J' : GrothendieckTopology C'} {S : Sheaf J' RingCat.{u}}
2426	  [HasSheafify J' AddCommGrpCat.{u}] [J'.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
2427	  [J'.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]

2429	/-- Transport a generating family of `M` along a colimit-preserving functor `F` carrying
2430	the unit to the unit (`η : F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S`): the images
2431	`(mapFree F η σ.I).inv ≫ F.map σ.π` of the generators generate `F.obj M`, with the index
2432	type unchanged.
2433	
2434	The colimit-preservation hypothesis `hF` is taken as an *explicit* argument (rather than an
2435	instance) so that callers supply it directly: instance search for
2436	`PreservesColimitsOfSize` on the `def`-backed `Scheme.Modules` functors (e.g.
2437	`Scheme.Modules.pullback`) does not reliably fire through the `Modules` type abbreviation. -/
2438	noncomputable def map {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (σ : M.GeneratingSections)
2439	    (F : SheafOfModules.{u} R ⥤ SheafOfModules.{u} S)
2440	    (η : F.obj (SheafOfModules.unit R) ≅ SheafOfModules.unit S)
2441	    (hF : PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u} F) :
2442	    (F.obj M).GeneratingSections where
2443	  I := σ.I
2444	  s := (F.obj M).freeHomEquiv ((letI := hF; SheafOfModules.mapFree F η σ.I).inv ≫ F.map σ.π)
2445	  epi := by
2446	    letI := hF
2447	    letI : PreservesColimitsOfSize.{0, 0} F := preservesColimitsOfSize_shrink _
2448	    rw [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
2449	    haveI : Epi (F.map σ.π) := inferInstance
2450	    exact epi_comp _ _
2451	
2452	/-- The transported generating family keeps the index type `σ.I` definitionally. -/
2453	@[simp] theorem map_I {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (σ : M.GeneratingSections)
2454	    (F : SheafOfModules.{u} R ⥤ SheafOfModules.{u} S)
2455	    (η : F.obj (SheafOfModules.unit R) ≅ SheafOfModules.unit S)
2456	    (hF : PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u} F) :
2457	    (map σ F η hF).I = σ.I := rfl
2458	
2459	/-- The transported generating family is of finite type whenever `σ` is (the index type is
2460	unchanged). Stated as a theorem with explicit `hF` since `map` takes its colimit-preservation
2461	witness explicitly. -/
2462	theorem map_isFiniteType {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (σ : M.GeneratingSections) [σ.IsFiniteType]
2463	    (F : SheafOfModules.{u} R ⥤ SheafOfModules.{u} S)
2464	    (η : F.obj (SheafOfModules.unit R) ≅ SheafOfModules.unit S)
2465	    (hF : PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u} F) :
2466	    (map σ F η hF).IsFiniteType :=
2467	  ⟨inferInstanceAs (Finite σ.I)⟩

2684	/-! ## Generic flatness (Nitsure §4)
2685	
2686	Over a noetherian integral base `S`, a coherent sheaf on a finite-type
2687	`X ⟶ S` is flat above some non-empty open `V ⊆ S`. This is the inductive
2688	engine of the flattening-stratification theorem: combined with
2689	Noetherian induction on the closed complement `S ∖ V`, it produces the
2690	finite stratification of `S` by flatness loci.
2691	
2692	Algebraically (theorem `generic_flatness_algebraic`, no Lean pin): for a
2693	noetherian domain `A`, a finite-type `A`-algebra `B`, and a finite
2694	`B`-module `M`, there exists a non-zero `f ∈ A` such that `M_f` is a
2695	free `A_f`-module. The geometric form (this declaration) restricts to a
2696	non-empty affine open `Spec A ⊆ S` and applies the algebraic form on
2697	each finite-type-algebra patch of `X` above `Spec A`.
2698	
2699	Blueprint reference: `thm:generic_flatness` (Nitsure §4). -/
2700	
2701	/-! ### Project-local Mathlib supplement — G3 flat-locality sub-lemmas
2702	
2703	The flat-locality assembly `lem:gf_flat_locality_assembly` of the blueprint is
2704	synthesised from the four sub-results G3.1–G3.4 below over verified Mathlib
2705	flatness anchors.  Three of them are pure-algebra facts and are formalised here
2706	axiom-clean as stand-alone, reusable statements; the fourth (G3.2,
2707	`lem:gf_stalk_flat_over_base`) and the assembly itself are stated in the
2708	blueprint over *stalks* `F_x` of the sheaf of modules, an abstraction that
2709	Mathlib's `SheafOfModules` API does **not** yet provide (there is no
2710	`SheafOfModules.stalk` / `Scheme.Modules.stalk`).  See the handoff for the
2711	precise missing ingredients connecting these algebraic anchors to the geometric
2712	`genericFlatness` conclusion. -/
2713	
2714	/-- **G3.1 — free patch ⇒ flat patch** (`lem:gf_patch_free_imp_flat`).
2715	In the situation of `gf_flat_locality_assembly` each localized patch section
2716	module `(M_j)_f` is free over `A_f`, hence flat; this records the
2717	freeness-to-flatness passage as the thin wrapper over `Module.Flat.of_free`
2718	that the assembly consumes. Project-local: pins the blueprint sub-lemma. -/
2719	theorem gf_patch_free_imp_flat {R M : Type*} [CommRing R] [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
2720	    [Module.Free R M] : Module.Flat R M :=
2721	  Module.Flat.of_free
2722	
2723	/-- **G3.3 — flatness over a base is local on the source** (`lem:gf_flat_base_local_on_source`).
2724	Let `B` be an `R`-algebra and `N` a `B`-module, viewed as an `R`-module through
2725	the scalar tower `R → B → N`. If for every maximal ideal `Q ⊂ B` the
2726	localization `N_Q = LocalizedModule Q.primeCompl N` is flat over `R`, then `N`
2727	is flat over `R`.  This is the source-locality criterion: flatness over the
2728	fixed base `R` is detected at the maximal ideals of the *source* ring `B`
2729	(distinct from the base-maximal criterion `Module.flat_of_localized_maximal`).
2730	Project-local: instantiates `Module.flat_of_isLocalized_maximal` at the
2731	canonical prime localizations of `N`. -/

2733	    [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [Module B N] [IsScalarTower R B N]
2734	    (h : ∀ (Q : Ideal B) [Q.IsMaximal], Module.Flat R (LocalizedModule Q.primeCompl N)) :
2735	    Module.Flat R N :=
2736	  Module.flat_of_isLocalized_maximal B _ _ (fun _ _ => LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap _ _) h

2739	(`lem:gf_stalk_flat_localBase`).  Stalk-free algebraic core of the blueprint
2740	sub-lemma: if `R'` is a localization of `R` at a submonoid `S` (so `R'` is flat
2741	over `R`) and `N` is an `R'`-module that is flat over `R'`, then `N` is flat
2742	over `R`.  In the geometric application `R = 𝒪_{S,x}`, `R' = 𝒪_{S,p(y)}` (a
2743	localization of `R` because `p(y)` is a generization of `x`), and `N = F_y`;
2744	this upgrades "`F_y` flat over `𝒪_{S,p(y)}`" to "`F_y` flat over `𝒪_{S,x}`".

2746	theorem gf_stalk_flat_localBase {R R' N : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing R'] [Algebra R R']
2747	    (S : Submonoid R) [IsLocalization S R'] [AddCommGroup N] [Module R' N] [Module R N]
2748	    [IsScalarTower R R' N] (h : Module.Flat R' N) : Module.Flat R N :=
2749	  haveI : Module.Flat R R' := IsLocalization.flat R' S
2750	  Module.Flat.trans R R' N

2752	/-- **B1.0 — localization commutes with base tensor**
2753	(`lem:gf_localizedModule_baseChange_tensor_comm`).
2754	
2755	Let `R → B` be a ring map, `T ⊆ B` a submonoid, `N` a `B`-module (hence an `R`-module
2756	through the tower `R → B → N`), and `K` an `R`-module.  Then localising `N ⊗[R] K` at the
2757	source submonoid `T` is the same as localising `N` and then tensoring with `K`:
2758	`LocalizedModule T (N ⊗[R] K) ≃ₗ[R] (LocalizedModule T N) ⊗[R] K`.
2759	
2760	The construction uses that `(LocalizedModule T N) ⊗[R] K` is itself a localization of
2761	`N ⊗[R] K` at `T` (`IsLocalizedModule.rTensor`, the base-change identity
2762	`T⁻¹M ⊗[R] N = T⁻¹(M ⊗[R] N)`) and then transports the canonical localization through
2763	uniqueness of localizations (`IsLocalizedModule.iso`).  It is natural in `K` by construction
2764	(it is the `iso` of a natural transformation).  Project-local: pins the blueprint sub-lemma
2765	feeding `gf_flat_localizedModule_sameBase`. -/
2766	noncomputable def gf_localizedModule_baseChange_tensor_comm {R B N : Type*} [CommRing R]
2767	    [CommRing B] [Algebra R B] [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [Module B N] [IsScalarTower R B N]
2768	    (T : Submonoid B) (K : Type*) [AddCommGroup K] [Module R K] :
2769	    LocalizedModule T (TensorProduct R N K) ≃ₗ[R] TensorProduct R (LocalizedModule T N) K :=
2770	  (IsLocalizedModule.iso T
2771	    (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.rTensor R K
2772	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap T N))).restrictScalars R
2773	
2774	/-- **B1 — localizing the source ring preserves flatness over the base**
2775	(`lem:gf_flat_localizedModule_sameBase`).
2776	
2777	Let `R → B → N` be a scalar tower with `N` a `B`-module, and `T ⊆ B` a submonoid of the
2778	*source* ring `B`.  Viewing `N` as an `R`-module through the tower, if `N` is flat over `R`
2779	then `LocalizedModule T N` is again flat over `R`.
2780	
2781	Unlike Mathlib's `Module.Flat.localizedModule` / `Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule`, which
2782	localize over a submonoid of the *base* `R`, here the submonoid lives in the source `B` while
2783	the base `R` is untouched.  Proof: flatness is checked by `Module.Flat.iff_lTensor_injectiveₛ`;
2784	for an `R`-submodule `N₀ ≤ P` the map `(N₀.subtype).lTensor (LocalizedModule T N)` is, via the
2785	base-tensor/localization commutation `IsLocalizedModule.map_lTensor`
2786	(cf. `gf_localizedModule_baseChange_tensor_comm`), the `T`-localization of
2787	`(N₀.subtype).lTensor N`; the latter is injective because `N` is `R`-flat
2788	(`Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap`) and `T`-localization preserves
2789	injectivity (`IsLocalizedModule.map_injective`, exactness of localization).  Project-local: the
2790	single genuine Mathlib gap of the flat-locality assembly. -/
2791	theorem gf_flat_localizedModule_sameBase {R B N : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing B]
2792	    [Algebra R B] [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [Module B N] [IsScalarTower R B N]
2793	    (T : Submonoid B) [Module.Flat R N] :
2794	    Module.Flat R (LocalizedModule T N) := by
2795	  rw [Module.Flat.iff_lTensor_injectiveₛ]
2796	  intro P _ _ N₀
2797	  have key := IsLocalizedModule.map_lTensor (R := R) (S := T)
2798	      (M := N) (M' := LocalizedModule T N) (g := LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap T N)
2799	      (N := N₀) (P := P) (f := N₀.subtype)
2800	  rw [← TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.coe_lTensor (A := B), ← key]
2801	  apply IsLocalizedModule.map_injective
2802	  rw [TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.coe_lTensor]
2803	  exact Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap _ Subtype.val_injective

2805	/-! ### Geometric B2 chain feeding the flat-locality assembly (blueprint §B2)
2806	
2807	The four lemmas `gf_crossChart_basicOpen_eq` (B2.1), `gf_section_localization_twoleg`
2808	(B2.2), `gf_base_localization_comparison` (B2.3), `gf_crossChart_spanning_cover`
2809	(B2.4) supply the geometric plumbing of `gf_flat_locality_assembly`, which in turn

2811	
2812	/-- **B2.1 — cross-chart basic-open identity** (`lem:gf_crossChart_basicOpen_eq`).
2813	Two sections `g ∈ Γ(X, W)`, `ḡ ∈ Γ(X, Wⱼ)` on overlapping opens that restrict to the
2814	*same* section over the overlap `O = W ⊓ Wⱼ`, and whose basic opens both lie inside `O`,
2815	cut out the *same* open `X.basicOpen g = X.basicOpen ḡ`. Pure geometry over
2816	`Scheme.basicOpen_res`: `X.basicOpen (g|_O) = O ⊓ X.basicOpen g`, which collapses to
2817	`X.basicOpen g` since `X.basicOpen g ≤ O`; the matching hypothesis equates the two
2818	left-hand sides. Project-local: pins the blueprint sub-lemma. -/
2819	theorem gf_crossChart_basicOpen_eq {X : Scheme.{u}} {W Wj : X.Opens}
2820	    (g : Γ(X, W)) (gbar : Γ(X, Wj))
2821	    (hg : X.basicOpen g ≤ W ⊓ Wj) (hgbar : X.basicOpen gbar ≤ W ⊓ Wj)
2822	    (hmatch : X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (inf_le_left : W ⊓ Wj ≤ W)).op g
2823	            = X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (inf_le_right : W ⊓ Wj ≤ Wj)).op gbar) :
2824	    X.basicOpen g = X.basicOpen gbar := by
2825	  have h1 : X.basicOpen (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (inf_le_left : W ⊓ Wj ≤ W)).op g)
2826	          = (W ⊓ Wj) ⊓ X.basicOpen g := X.basicOpen_res g _
2827	  have h2 : X.basicOpen (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (inf_le_right : W ⊓ Wj ≤ Wj)).op gbar)
2828	          = (W ⊓ Wj) ⊓ X.basicOpen gbar := X.basicOpen_res gbar _
2829	  rw [hmatch, h2, inf_eq_right.mpr hgbar] at h1
2830	  rw [inf_eq_right.mpr hg] at h1
2831	  exact h1.symm

2836	both `(powers g)⁻¹ Γ(F, W)` (leg 1) and `(powers ḡ)⁻¹ Γ(F, Wⱼ) = (Mⱼ)_ḡ` (leg 2).  Both legs

2839	`(powers g)⁻¹ Γ(F, W) ≅ (Mⱼ)_ḡ`.  Project-local: pins the blueprint sub-lemma. -/
2840	theorem gf_section_localization_twoleg {X : Scheme.{u}} (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
2841	    {W Wj : X.Opens} (hW : IsAffineOpen W) (hWj : IsAffineOpen Wj)
2842	    (g : Γ(X, W)) (gbar : Γ(X, Wj))
2843	    [Module Γ(X, W) Γ(F, X.basicOpen g)]
2844	    [IsScalarTower Γ(X, W) Γ(X, X.basicOpen g) Γ(F, X.basicOpen g)]
2845	    [Module Γ(X, Wj) Γ(F, X.basicOpen gbar)]
2846	    [IsScalarTower Γ(X, Wj) Γ(X, X.basicOpen gbar) Γ(F, X.basicOpen gbar)] :
2847	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) (Scheme.Modules.restrictBasicOpenₗ F g) ∧
2848	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers gbar) (Scheme.Modules.restrictBasicOpenₗ F gbar) :=
2849	  ⟨Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen F hW g,
2850	   Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen F hWj gbar⟩

2852	/-- **B2.3 — base comparison: `Γ(S, U)` is flat over `A_f`**
2853	(`lem:gf_base_localization_comparison`).
2854	For affine opens `U ≤ V` of a scheme `S`, the restriction `Γ(S, V) → Γ(S, U)` makes `Γ(S, U)`
2855	flat over `Γ(S, V)`.  Geometrically this is the flatness of the open immersion of affines
2856	`Spec Γ(S, U) ≅ U ↪ V ≅ Spec Γ(S, V)` (`AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.instOfIsOpenImmersion`), read on
2857	section rings via the `Flat.mk` affine-local criterion.  In the generic-flatness application
2858	`V = D(f) = Spec A_f` and `U ≤ V` is the affine open over which the base ring is `R = Γ(S, U)`;
2859	this is the weakened (flat) form of the blueprint sub-lemma — all the assembly consumes.
2860	Project-local: pins the blueprint sub-lemma. -/
2861	theorem gf_base_localization_comparison {S : Scheme.{u}}
2862	    {V U : S.Opens} (hV : IsAffineOpen V) (hU : IsAffineOpen U) (e : U ≤ V) :
2863	    letI : Algebra Γ(S, V) Γ(S, U) := (S.presheaf.map (homOfLE e).op).hom.toAlgebra
2864	    Module.Flat Γ(S, V) Γ(S, U) := by
2865	  -- The identity `𝟙 S` is a flat morphism (it is an open immersion); its `appLE V U` is the
2866	  -- restriction ring map `Γ(S, V) → Γ(S, U)`, so flatness of the morphism gives flatness of the
2867	  -- ring map, which is definitionally `Module.Flat Γ(S, V) Γ(S, U)` for the restriction algebra.
2868	  have e' : U ≤ (𝟙 S) ⁻¹ᵁ V := by simpa using e
2869	  have hF : (Scheme.Hom.appLE (𝟙 S) V U e').hom.Flat :=
2870	    Flat.flat_appLE (𝟙 S) hV hU e'
2871	  have happ : Scheme.Hom.appLE (𝟙 S) V U e' = S.presheaf.map (homOfLE e).op := by
2872	    rw [Scheme.Hom.appLE, Scheme.Hom.id_app]
2873	    exact Category.id_comp _
2874	  rw [happ] at hF
2875	  exact hF

2877	/-- **Per-point common basic open** (geometric basis lemma feeding B2.4).
2878	For two affine opens `W, Wᵢ` of a scheme `X` and a point `x ∈ W ⊓ Wᵢ`, there is a section
2879	`g ∈ Γ(X, W)` and a partner `ḡ ∈ Γ(X, Wᵢ)` with `x ∈ D(g)`, both basic opens inside the overlap
2880	`W ⊓ Wᵢ`, that cut out the *same* open `X.basicOpen g = X.basicOpen ḡ`.  This is the statement
2881	that the opens simultaneously basic in `W` and in `Wᵢ` form a basis of `W ∩ Wᵢ`.  Construction:
2882	pick a basic open `D(b)` of `Wᵢ` inside the overlap around `x` (`IsAffineOpen.exists_basicOpen_le`
2883	in chart `Wᵢ`), then a basic open `D(g)` of `W` inside `D(b)` (in chart `W`); finally realise
2884	`D(g)` back as a basic open `D(ḡ)` of `Wᵢ` by writing the restriction `g|_{D(b)}` through the
2885	localization `Γ(X, D(b)) ≅ (Γ(X, Wᵢ))_b` (`IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_basicOpen`) as `ḡ'/bⁿ` and
2886	taking `ḡ := b · ḡ'`, so `D(ḡ) = D(b) ⊓ D(ḡ') = D(g)`.
2887	
2888	NOTE (iter-054 prover finding): the blueprint/B2.1 *restriction-matched* datum `g|_O = ḡ|_O` is
2889	**not constructible** in general — the realisation above only yields `ḡ = (unit)·g` on the
2890	overlap, so the achievable invariant is the basic-open equality `X.basicOpen g = X.basicOpen ḡ`
2891	(which is all B2.2/the assembly consume; `D(g) = D(ḡ)` makes `Γ(F, D(g))` one group).  The
2892	conclusion here is therefore stated as the basic-open equality, not the restriction equality.
2893	Project-local: the genuine geometric crux; the cross-chart realisation of a basic open of one
2894	affine as a basic open of the other is not packaged in Mathlib. -/
2895	theorem gf_common_basicOpen_basis {X : Scheme.{u}} {W Wi : X.Opens}
2896	    (hW : IsAffineOpen W) (hWi : IsAffineOpen Wi) (x : X) (hxW : x ∈ W) (hxWi : x ∈ Wi) :
2897	    ∃ (g : Γ(X, W)) (gbar : Γ(X, Wi)),
2898	      x ∈ X.basicOpen g ∧ X.basicOpen g ≤ W ⊓ Wi ∧ X.basicOpen gbar ≤ W ⊓ Wi ∧
2899	      X.basicOpen g = X.basicOpen gbar := by
2900	  -- The cross-chart realisation is packaged in Mathlib as
2901	  -- `exists_basicOpen_le_affine_inter`: at a point `x ∈ W ⊓ Wᵢ` of two affine opens it
2902	  -- produces sections `g ∈ Γ(X, W)`, `ḡ ∈ Γ(X, Wᵢ)` cutting out the *same* basic open
2903	  -- `D(g) = D(ḡ)` with `x ∈ D(g)`.  Its construction is exactly the recipe documented above
2904	  -- (`IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_basicOpen` + `IsLocalization.surj''` + `basicOpen_mul`).
2905	  -- The containment `D(g) ≤ W ⊓ Wᵢ` then follows from `X.basicOpen_le` on both charts plus
2906	  -- the common-open equality.
2907	  obtain ⟨g, gbar, hbo, hx_mem⟩ :=
2908	    exists_basicOpen_le_affine_inter hW hWi x ⟨hxW, hxWi⟩
2909	  have hgW : X.basicOpen g ≤ W := X.basicOpen_le g
2910	  have hgbarWi : X.basicOpen gbar ≤ Wi := X.basicOpen_le gbar
2911	  exact ⟨g, gbar, hx_mem, le_inf hgW (hbo.symm ▸ hgbarWi),
2912	    le_inf (hbo ▸ hgW) hgbarWi, hbo⟩
2913	
2914	/-- **B2.4 — finite spanning basic-open cover aligned to the patches**
2915	(`lem:gf_crossChart_spanning_cover`).  For an affine open `W` of a scheme `X` and a family
2916	`{Wⱼ}` of affine opens covering `W`, there is a finite family `t ⊆ Γ(X, W)` with
2917	`Ideal.span t = ⊤` such that each `g ∈ t` comes with a chart index `i` and a partner
2918	`ḡ ∈ Γ(X, Wᵢ)`: `D(g)` and `D(ḡ)` both lie in the overlap `W ⊓ Wᵢ` and cut out the same open
2919	`D(g) = D(ḡ)`.  Assembled from the per-point common-basic-open basis `gf_common_basicOpen_basis`
2920	and the affine quasi-compact span extraction (`IsAffineOpen.self_le_iSup_basicOpen_iff` +
2921	`Ideal.span_eq_top_iff_finite`).  Project-local: the geometric crux of the flat-locality
2922	assembly. -/
2923	theorem gf_crossChart_spanning_cover {X : Scheme.{u}} {W : X.Opens} (hW : IsAffineOpen W)
2924	    {ι : Type*} (Wj : ι → X.Opens) (hWj : ∀ i, IsAffineOpen (Wj i))
2925	    (hcov : W ≤ ⨆ i, Wj i) :
2926	    ∃ (t : Finset Γ(X, W)), Ideal.span (t : Set Γ(X, W)) = ⊤ ∧
2927	      ∀ g ∈ t, ∃ (i : ι) (gbar : Γ(X, Wj i)),
2928	        X.basicOpen g ≤ W ⊓ Wj i ∧ X.basicOpen gbar ≤ W ⊓ Wj i ∧
2929	        X.basicOpen g = X.basicOpen gbar := by
2930	  classical
2931	  -- For each point of `W`, choose a chart `Wᵢ ∋ x` and a common basic open `D(g) ∋ x` cut out
2932	  -- also by a partner `ḡ ∈ Γ(X, Wᵢ)` (the geometric basis lemma `gf_common_basicOpen_basis`).

3001	`lem:gf_base_localization_comparison` / `lem:gf_section_localization_flat_descent`).

3003	Let `A → R` be a ring map and `M` an `R`-module that, through the scalar tower `A → R → M`, is
3004	flat over `A`.  If the identity `M →ₗ[A] M` exhibits the `R`-module `M` as the base change of the
3005	flat `A`-module `M` along `A → R` (equivalently, the canonical map `R ⊗[A] M → M` is an
3006	isomorphism — the situation when `A → R` is a ring *epimorphism*, e.g. the section map of an open
3007	immersion of affines `Spec R = U ↪ V = Spec A`), then `M` is flat over `R`.

3010	*localization* of `A = Γ(S,V)` (so `Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule` does not apply), but `U ↪ V`
